questions.question: If the humidity in the air increases then the rate of evaporation:-
questions.explanation: Because the water evaporates very slowly due to muggy (damp) weather.

questions.question: Which of the following statement is correct and why?

options.option: boiling is a surface phenomenon and evaporation is a bulk phenomenon
boiling is a bulk phenomenon and evaporation is a surface phenomenon
boiling and surface both are bulk phenomena
boiling and evaporation both are a surface phenomenon

questions.explanation:
At the boiling point, the particles of a liquid have sufficient energy to overcome the forces of attraction holding them together and separate into individual particles. And the liquid boils to form a gas.
some particles in a liquid always have more kinetic energy than the others. so, even when a liquid is well below its boiling point, some of its particles have enough energy to break the forces of attraction between the particles and escape from the surface of the liquid in the form of vapour (gas).

questions.question: If the temperature of a place increases then evaporation:-
questions.explanation: If the temperature of the place is increased due to this the temperature of a liquid is also increased, more number of particles of the liquid get enough kinetic energy to go into the vapour state. This increases the rate of evaporation with the increase in temperature.

questions.question: Which of the following have the least interatomic spacing?
questions.explanation: This is because the force of attraction between the particles (or inter-particles forces) is stronger in solids, less in liquids and negligible in gases and the movement of particles ( or kinetic energy of particles ) is the minimum in solids, more in liquids and the maximum in gases.

questions.question: If you decrease the surface area and increase the temperature, then the rate of evaporation

options.option: may increase or decrease depending upon other factors

questions.explanation: This is because on increasing the temperature the rate of evaporation increases but at the same time surface area decreases and hence the rate of evaporation decreases. Due to this, the temperature remains the same.

questions.question: 300 K will have its corresponding temperature in degree celsius as:-
questions.explanation:
To convert a temperature on the Kelvin scale to the Celsius scale, we have to subtract 273 from the Kelvin temperature.
Temperature on Celsius scale = Temperature on the Kelvin scale - 273
= 300 - 273
= 27o C

questions.question: Liquid to gas and gas to liquid changes are called:-
questions.explanation:
At the boiling point, particles of a liquid have sufficient kinetic energy (K.E) to overcome the forces of attraction holding them together and separate into individual particles then that liquid boils to form a gas. (vaporization)
When a gas-cooled enough, then its particles lose so much kinetic energy (K.E) that they slow down, move closer together until they start being attracted to each other, and that forms a liquid. (condensation)

questions.question: The physical state of water at 25oC, 0oC, and 100oC is respectively.
questions.explanation:
At 25oC particles of water are in a liquid state.
At 0oC particles of water stop moving and vibrate about a fixed position. At this stage, the water freezes and becomes solid.
At 100oC particles of water have sufficient kinetic energy to overcome the forces of attraction holding them together and separate into individual particles. and the liquid boils to form a gas.

questions.question: Which is compressible in nature?
questions.explanation: Sponge is compressible in nature due to the presence of air cavities filled with air. Even though sponge has definite shape it can be compressed easily. The molecules inside the sponge are loosely packed and air is present between them. When sponge is compressed the air comes out making it soft solid substance.

questions.question: On the basis of composition, matter is classified as
questions.explanation: On the basis of composition, matter is classified as element, compound and mixture. If a chemical process such as combustion or oxidation breaks the substance to its constituent atoms then it is a compound eg salt otherwise the substance is an element. Compounds are made up of single type of atoms. If a physical process like evaporation occurs to separate it then sample is called mixture.

questions.question: The temperature above which a gas cannot be liquefied is known as:
questions.explanation: The critical temperature of a substance is the temperature at and above which vapor of the substance cannot be liquefied, no matter how much pressure is applied.

questions.question: In which one of the following sets, is each one a solid under ordinary conditions?
questions.explanation: Out of the given sets of substances, salt, sugar, and chalk are solids under ordinary temperature. Mercury, water, oxygen, and nitrogen are not solids. Mercury and water are liquid at room temperature. Oxygen and nitrogen are gases.

questions.question: The inter-particle forces are the strongest in
questions.explanation: Solids substances have the strongest inter-particle force of attraction. In the given substances, sodium bromide is solid, ethyl alcohol is liquid, and ammonia and carbon dioxide are gases. So, the particles of Sodium bromide have the strongest force of attraction.

questions.question: Pressure on the surface of a gas is increased. What will happen to the inter-particle forces?

options.option: The inter-particle forces of attraction will remain constant.

questions.explanation: When the pressure increases on the particles of gas then that these particles come together and their intermolecular attraction force increases due to which the gaseous form is converted into liquid form.

questions.question: The melting points of two solids [A] and [B] are 300 K and 350 K respectively. Which has stronger inter-particle forces?
questions.explanation: The melting point of a solid is an indication of the strength of the force of attraction between its particles. So, solid B has the more intermolecular force of attraction.

questions.question:
What are the characteristics of the particles of matter?

  1. Particles of matter are in continuous motion.
  2. Particles of matters do not have spaces between them.
  3. Particles of matter attract each other.
  4. Particles of matter are very large in size.
questions.explanation:
Characteristics of Particles of Matter

  1. Particles of matter have space between them – When we dissolve sugar, salt or potassium permanganate in water, particles get evenly distributed in the water. Particles of one type of matter diffuse into space between particles of the other.
  2. Particles of matter are continuously moving – Particles of matter are continuously moving, they possess kinetic energy which increases with an increase in temperature.
  3. Particles of matter attract each other – Particles of matter have a force acting between them, these forces keep the particles together. The strength of this force of attraction varies from one kind of matter to another.

questions.question: A substance upon heating directly changes into a gaseous state. What is this change called?
questions.explanation: A change of state directly from solid to gaseous without changing into liquid or vice versa is called Sublimation.

questions.question: A gas can be best liquefied:

options.option: By increasing the pressure and reducing the temperature.
By increasing the temperature and reducing the pressure.

questions.explanation: The physical state of matter is defined by the intermolecular spaces between them. The gases have the highest empty space between them while the liquids have comparatively lower space. Hence the gases can be liquefied by reducing the gap between the molecules. This can be done either by reducing the temperature or increasing the pressure.

questions.question: The evaporation of a liquid occurs only at
questions.explanation: The process of changing a liquid into gases (vapour) even below its boiling point is called evaporation. The rate of evaporation increase with the increase in temperature, surface area, and wind speed.

questions.question: Which of the following factors are responsible for the change of state of solid CO2 into vapours?

options.option: Both decrease in pressure and an increase in temperature

questions.explanation: Solid carbon dioxide can be changed into vapours or gaseous state by decreasing the pressure and/or increasing the temperature. Both processes increase the spaces between the constituent particles. They also increase the kinetic energy of the particles.

questions.question: The melting point of a substance is defined as the constant temperature at atmospheric pressure when:

options.option: the solid is completely changed into a liquid.

questions.explanation: Melting point is defined as the constant temperature at which the solid and the liquid phases of a substance coexist. The melting point of a solid is an indication of the strength of the force of attraction between its particle.

questions.question: Which one of the following decreases the extent of evaporation of water?
questions.explanation: Evaporation of water increase with the increase in temperature, surface area, and wind speed but decreases with an increase in humidity.

questions.question: A desert cooler gave comfort due to cooling caused by the evaporation of water. Under which one of the following conditions it work more effectively?
questions.explanation: A desert cooler cools better on a hot and dry day because on a hot dry day temperature is high and humidity is less which helps in better evaporation. Due to the higher rate of evaporation, it gives a better cooling effect.

questions.question: When alcohol is kept in a china dish it evaporates, the temperature falls and cooling is produced. Which one is the correct method to record the temperature?

options.option: By touching the surface of china dish
By taking the help of the teacher.

questions.explanation: By touching the surface of the china dish. This is due to the fact that to change from liquid to the vapour state, alcohol requires latent heat of vaporisation. the alcohol takes this heat of vaporization from china dish. The china dish loses heat and gets cooled.

questions.question: In an endothermic process, heat is absorbed, in an exothermic process heat is evolved and in an athermic process, no thermal change is observed. What is the nature of evaporation of ether?
questions.explanation: Evaporation of ether occurs due to the absorption of heat by surrounding. So, it is an endothermic process. The chemical reaction in which heat is absorbed is called an endothermic reaction. The reaction in which heat is evolved is called exothermic reaction and the reaction in which no thermal changes occur is called the athermic process.

questions.question: Which of the following solids undergo sublimation upon heating?
questions.explanation: The process of changing solid directly into gases without changing into a liquid is called sublimation. sugar, urea, and ice are not sublime substances, Iodine, ammonium chloride, naphthalene balls, camphor is a sublime substance.

questions.question: When the liquid starts boiling, the further heat energy which is supplied

options.option: increases the K.E of the particle in the liquid
is absorbed as latent heat of vaporization by the liquid
is lost to the surrounding as such

questions.explanation: The latent heat does not increase the kinetic energy of the particles of the substance. and since there is no increase in the kinetic energy of the particles, the temp. of a substance does not rise during the change of state. When the liquid starts boiling, the further heat energy which is supplied is absorbed as latent heat of vaporization by the liquid to change its state of matter.

questions.question: Name a process by which an impure sample of naphthalene can be purified.
questions.explanation: During sublimation, naphthalene being sublime changes into vapors leaving behind impurities. Vapour can be cooled by condensation. The impure sample of naphthalene can be purified by the sublimation process.

questions.question: A liquid is kept in an open china dish A. The evaporation of the liquid can be accelerated.

options.option: By keeping the dish in the open
By keeping the dish under a running fan

questions.explanation: All the statements are correct because the rate of evaporation of liquid can be accelerated by increasing the surface area, increasing wind speed, and increasing the temperature.

questions.question: Which of the following energy is absorbed during the change of state of a substance?
questions.explanation: During the change of state of a substance, latent heat is absorbed. The latent heat does not raise the temperature so there is no increase in the kinetic energy of the particles of the substance.

questions.question: Diethyl ether evaporates faster than ethyl alcohol because of which factor?

options.option: Boiling point of ether is higher than that of alcohol.
Molar mass of ether is graters than that of ether.
The Vapour pressure of ether is greater than that of alcohol.
vapour pressure of alcohol is greater than that of ether

questions.explanation: Being lower vapour pressure ether starts evaporating faster than alcohol. Diethyl ether evaporates faster than that of ethyl alcohol because the vapour pressure of ether is greater than that of alcohol.

questions.question:
An equal volume of ethanol is kept separately in a test tube, in a china dish, in a water glass, and in a conical flask.
In which container alcohol evaporates faster?
questions.explanation: The rate of evaporation depends upon the surface area available for evaporation. If the equal volume of ethanol is kept in a test tube, china dish, water glass, and conical flask, the alcohol evaporates faster in China dish having a greater surface area.

questions.question: When acetone or nail polish remover is applied cooling is experienced due to which factor?
questions.explanation: Evaporation causes a cooling effect because the heat of evaporation is absorbed by the surroundings. Evaporation of acetone present in nail polish remover absorbs the latent heat from the nail as well as surrounding causing cooling of a nail.

questions.question:
Water kept in an earthen pot becomes cool in summer, because:

  1. Molecules of water escape through pores and cause cooling.
  2. Earthen pots are made up of clay.
  3. It decreases the temperature of the water.
  4. In summer, the rate of evaporation of water is faster.
questions.explanation:
Water kept in an earthen pot (matka) becomes cool in summer Because

  1. Pot surface made of earthen clay has a large number of extremely small pores in its walls. Some water continuously seeps through the porous walls to the outside of the earthen pot.
  2.  During evaporation, water absorbs the latent heat required for vaporization from the surface of an earthen pot, the remaining water, and the surrounding air.
  3. Heat energy equal to the latent heat of vaporization is absorbed from the earthen pot leaving the water cooler than before means temperature decreases.
  4. Evaporation is a surface phenomenon, and it causes a cooling effect.

So, statement (A), (B), (C), and (D) are correct.

questions.question: The two major gases present in the air are
questions.explanation: Air is a mixture of gases. Two main constituents of air are Nitrogen (78%) and Oxygen (21%). Other gases include carbon dioxide, helium, water vapour, etc.

questions.question: Three pieces of cloth were soaked in water, alcohol, and ether separately and kept on a table. What is the decreasing order of drying of the cloths?
questions.explanation: The decreasing order of the rate of drying is Water < Alcohol < Ether. The cloth soaked in water will take longer time in drying as water is less volatile compared to alcohol and ether. A cloth soaked in alcohol is will take less time than that soaked in water. The cloth soaked in ether will dry quickly as ether is a highly volatile liquid. The particles of ether gain energy from the cloth and/or surroundings and evaporate quickly.

questions.question: Sulphur dioxide is collected in the lab in a gas jar by upward displacement of air. Which of the following statement is correct regarding the density of the gas?

options.option: Its density is equal to that of air.
No correct statement can be made about the density of the gas.

questions.explanation: Gases are collected in different ways, depending upon their density and solubility in water. Sulphur dioxide gas is collected in the lab in a gas jar by upward displacement of the air because sulphur dioxide (SO2) gas is heavier (denser) than air. Its relative density is 1.43. Sulphur dioxide gas produced in a chemical reaction is passed through a delivery tube into a gas jar, where it sinks and pushes the air out of the top.

questions.question: Which of the following is needed by surgeons during surgery?
questions.explanation: Ether is used by surgeons during surgery. Ether is highly volatile and antiseptic in nature. It is used to make a wound sterile. It was one of the first anesthetics used in surgery.

questions.question:
Which of the following is not a chemical change?

  1. Changing of milk into curd
  2. Freezing of water
  3. Burning of paper
  4. Mixing of iron filling & sand
questions.explanation: Because in both cases no new substance with new chemical properties is formed.

questions.question: In the determination of boiling of water, it is advised to put the bulb of the thermometer above the water rather than in water, it is to:

options.option: make sure that boiling point obtained is accurate even when water sample contains non-volatile impurities dissolved.
reduce the error due to expansions of glass because of heat.
reduce the error due to atmospheric pressure.
obtain the boiling point accurately even in much a shorter time.

questions.explanation: If the bulb of the thermometer touches the water then it would show the temperature of the water in the surface, not the waters boiling temperature. so it is advised that the bulb of the thermometer shouldn't touch the boiling water. Nonvolatile impurity will not repair therefore, we can find accurate boiling of water. Nonvolatile impurities increase the boiling point of the solution.

questions.question: Boiling point of water is :
questions.explanation: The boiling point is the temperature at which boiling occurs for a specific liquid. For example, for water, the boiling point is 100ºC at a pressure of 1 atm.

questions.question: When water boils its temperature

options.option: keeps on increasing as long as heating is continued.
may decreases or increases depending on the place where the experiment is being carried out.

questions.explanation: The temperature remains constant during the boiling of water even though heat is supplied continuously. This is because the heat supplied is absorbed by the water particles and this heat increases their kinetic energy. Thus, because of an increase in kinetic energy, the bond between the water particles is cut down and they move more freely, compared to water. Hence, they become gas. So, this is why the temperature remains constant even though heat is supplied continuously to the water.

questions.question: Name the phenomenon which causes one crystal of potassium permanganate to turn a beaker of water purple.
questions.explanation: Diffusion is a mass transfer phenomenon that causes the distribution of a chemical species to become more uniform in space as time passes. Potassium permanganate diffusion in water. The beaker containing potassium permanganate (purple) and water, and a clock being used to time how long it takes for the purple colour to spread through the water as the potassium permanganate dissolves. This apparatus is used to demonstrate diffusion in a liquid. Eventually, the random motion of all the potassium permanganate particles results in the purple colour being equally dispersed throughout the water. The process appears slow as the dissolved particles collide with the water molecules and each other, slowing their progress.

questions.question: Liquid A boils at 60oC while liquid B boils at 80oC which is more volatile?
questions.explanation:
Boiling Point of liquid A = 60oC
Boiling Point of liquid B = 80oC
Liquid A has a lower boiling point, so it is being more volatile and has a high vapor pressure at 80oC Because vapour pressure has an inverse relation with the boiling point.

questions.question: Water in a container is heated uniformly from 0oC to 100oC. Then volume :
questions.explanation: The particles (Molecules) of frozen H2O have air space between them. These air spaces decrease the density of ice increases when the temperature increase from 0°C to 4° C. In this way, the volume of Ice decrease between these temperatures. And when the temperature further increases from 4° C to 10° C or 20° C. The particles get more energy and the start moving faster and collide with each other and in this way the space between them increases and their volume increases.

questions.question: A pressure cooker work on the basis of which of the following principle?

options.option: Boiling point is raised by increasing the pressure on the surface of the liquid.
Boiling point is lowered by increasing the pressure on the surface of the liquid.

questions.explanation: Pressure cookers work on the principle that as the pressure increases the boiling point of water increases and the steam is produced from water. The pressure cookers are designed in such a way it controls the escape of steam. When the steam is not allowed to escape, it builds up more pressure which allows the water in the cooker to start boiling with an increase in temperature. Thus higher the temperature of the water makes cooking faster. And the steam covering the entire cooker completely helps to heat and cook food evenly and quickly.

questions.question: In the experiment, determination of melting point of Ice, the reading of the thermometer must be noted when:
questions.explanation: At a particular temperature, the water starts boiling and this constant temperature is the boiling point. So, the reading of the thermometer must be noted when the temperature becomes constant.

questions.question: When we observe the melting of ice, the melting point of ice is a constant temperature at which
questions.explanation: A stage is reached when solid starts melting to become a liquid at atmospheric pressure is called its Melting point. So, when we observe the melting of ice, the melting point of ice is a constant temperature at which ice and water both are present.

questions.question: Which of the following set of apparatus is required to determine the boiling point of water?

options.option: Funnel, burner, clamp and stand, test tube, thermometer, wire gauze
Boiling tube, beaker, thermometer, burner, cork with one hole, stand with clamp, wire gauze
Tripod stand, conical flask, thermometer, wire gauze, stand with clamp, pair of tongs
Round bottom flask, burner, thermometer, wire gauze, stand with clamp, cork with two holes, glass tube

questions.explanation: Round bottom flask, burner, thermometer, wire gauze, stand with clam, cork with two holes, glass tube are needed.

questions.question: In the determination of boiling point of water correct reading in the thermometer is noted when :
questions.explanation: Boiling point: The temperature at which the liquid boils and changes into gases state at the atmospheric pressure is called the boiling point. In the determination of boiling point of water, correct reading on the thermometer is noted when the temperature becomes constant.

questions.question: At 0oC or 273 K, the physical state of water is observed as :
questions.explanation: Zero on the Celsius scale (0o C) is now defined as the equivalent to 273K, with a temperature difference of 1 deg C equivalent to a difference of 1 K, meaning the unit size in each scale is the same. This means that 100o C, previously defined as the boiling point of water, is now defined as the equivalent to 373 K. At 0o C or 273K the water is at its freezing point, hence both solid and liquid phases are present.

questions.question: When water boils its temperature remains the ________.
questions.explanation: The temperature remains constant during boiling of water even though heat is supplied constantly because all the heat energy provided is used up in changing the state of water from liquid to gaseous water vapour. Therefore all the heat energy provided increases the kinetic energy of the particles and temperature doesn't increase.

questions.question: The correct set-up for finding the melting point of ice is:
questions.explanation: This is the correct set-up for finding the melting point of ice, because to find the melting point of ice the pin of thermometer should be dipped into ice.

questions.question: Which one of the following sets of phenomena would increase on raising the temperature?
questions.explanation: • As temperature increases, the kinetic energy of gases increases, and molecules will move faster than earlier. Hence, the molecule will have high evaporation, diffusion, expansion of gases as all these properties are directly proportional to temperature.

questions.question: During summer, water kept in an earthen pot becomes cool because of the phenomenon of
questions.explanation:
During summer, water kept in an earthen pot becomes cool because of the phenomenon of evaporation.

Earthen pot has a large number of tiny pores in its walls. So, some of the water molecules continuously keep seeping through these pores outside the pot. This water evaporates continuously and takes the latent heat required for vaporization from the remaining water. In this way, the remaining water loses heat and gets cooled.

questions.question: A few substances are arranged in the increasing order of forces of attraction between their particles. Which one of the following represents a correct arrangement?
questions.explanation:
Intermolecular forces are forces of attraction or repulsion which act between neighboring particles (atoms, molecules, or ions). These forces keep a molecule together.
In solid, a particle can vibrate but they can not move. Sugar is solid.
In liquid, the force of attraction is weaker than solid. Water is a liquid.
In gas, the particle moves fast in a random direction so there is no force of attraction. Oxygen is a gas.
Therefore, the increasing order of forces of attraction is
Oxygen < water < sugar

questions.question: On converting 25°C, 38°C and 66°C to Kelvin scale, the correct sequence of temperature will be
questions.explanation:
Kelvin is the SI unit of temperature. On converting 25°C, 38°C and 66°C, to Kelvin scale, we get the following temperatures
⇒ 25°C + 273 = 298 K
⇒ 38°C + 273 = 311 K
⇒ 66°C + 273 = 339 K
Therefore, the correct sequence of temperatures will be 298 K, 311 K, and 339 K.

questions.question: The boiling points of diethyl ether, acetone, and n-butyl alcohol are 35°C, 56°C, and 118°C respectively. Which one of the following correctly represents their boiling points in the Kelvin scale?
questions.explanation:
Kelvin is the SI unit of temperature. The correct order of boiling points of diethyl ether, acetone and n-butyl alcohol in kelvin scale is
308 K, 329 K, and 391 K, which can be explained as (T°C + 273 = 7K).
Boiling point of diethyl ether = 35°C + 273 = 308 K
Boiling point of acetone = 56°C + 273 = 329 K
Boiling point of n-butyl alcohol = 118°C + 273 = 391 K

questions.question: Which condition out of the following will increase the evaporation of water?
questions.explanation: In evaporation when the temperature is increased, the molecules gain kinetic energy, and molecules start to move faster and in order to become a gas, it must overcome intermolecular forces between different molecules. Increasing the speed, molecules break free from bonds and evaporates.

questions.question:
In which of the following conditions, the distance between the molecules of hydrogen gas would increase?

  1. Increasing pressure on hydrogen contained in a closed container
  2. Some hydrogen gas leaking out of the container
  3. Increasing the volume of the container of hydrogen gas
  4. Adding more hydrogen gas to the container without increasing the volume of the container
questions.explanation: When hydrogen gas leaks out of the container, the molecules of the gas remaining in the container have more free space. Hence, the distance between the gas molecules increases. By increasing the volume of the container also increases the distance between the molecules.

questions.question:
A diver is able to cut through water in a swimming pool. It illustrates that

  1. Particles of liquids have space in between them
  2. Particles of liquids possess kinetic energy
  3. Particles of liquids have weak attractive forces.
  4. Both Particles of liquids have space in between them and Particles of liquids have weak attractive forces.
questions.explanation: Particles of liquids have space in between them and forces of attraction are weak.

questions.question: Which of the following represents the correct increasing order of the densities of given substances?

options.option: Air < Cotton < Exhaust from chimneys < Iron < Honey
Cotton < Exhaust from chimneys < Honey < Iron < Air
Cotton < Air < Exhaust from chimneys < Iron < Honey
Air < Exhaust from chimneys < Cotton < Honey < Iron

questions.explanation: Air < Exhaust from chimneys < Cotton < Honey < Iron

questions.question: Study the given heating curve of substance X carefully and select the correct statement.

options.option: Substance X exists in solid state at point 1, in liquid state at point 3 and in gaseous state at point 5.
At QR, matter exists both in solid and liquid states, while at ST matter exists both in liquid and gaseous states.
QR represents latent heat of fusion, while ST represents latent heat of vaporisation.

questions.explanation:
Below 273K (PQ), X exists in solid state. At 273 K (QR), interconversion of solid to liquid takes place. Above 273K (RS), X exists in liquid state. At 373 K (ST), interconversion of liquid to gas takes place. Above 373 K, X exists in gaseous state. At QR (273 K), interconversion of solid to liquid takes place so, matter exists both in solid and liquid states and it represents latent heat of fusion. At ST (373 K), interconversion of liquid to gas takes place so, matter exists in both liquid and gaseous states and it represents latent heat of vaporisation.
Temperature remains constant at QR and ST.

questions.question: Petroleum gas is filled in gas cylinders in liquid form. Under what conditions gases can be liquefied?
questions.explanation: Low temperature, high pressure

questions.question:
Which of the following statements are correct?

  1. Temperature changes during the change of a state.
  2. Dry ice gets converted directly into gaseous state under normal atmospheric conditions.
  3. Higher boiling point of liquid indicates weaker intermolecular forces.
  4. Latent heat of vaporisation is generally higher than the latent heat of fusion for a substance.
questions.explanation: Temperature remains constant during the change of state and higher boiling point of a liquid indicates stronger intermolecular forces.

questions.question:
Statement 1: Pieces of dry ice gradually get smaller when left at room temperature.
Statement 2: Dry ice undergoes sublimation.

options.option: Both statements 1 and 2 are true and statement 2 is the correct explanation of statement 1.
Both statements 1 and 2 are true but statement 2 is not the correct explanation of statement 1.
Statement 1 is true and statement 2 is false.

questions.explanation: Pieces of dry ice gradually get smaller when left at room temperature because dry ice undergoes sublimation. Dry ice sublimates at temperatures above -78.5 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.

questions.question:
Match column I with column II and select the correct option from the given codes.

Column I Column II
(a) Hot tea poured in saucer gets cooled faster.  (i) Evaporation increases with increase in temperature
(b) We feel more cold after a hot water bath than a cold water bath. (ii) Evaporation increases with increases with humidity.
(c) Water remains cool in earthen pitcher during dry hot day. (iii) Evaporation increases with increase in wind speed. 
(d) We feel comfortable under a moving fan in summer. (iv) Evaporation increases with increase in surface area.

Select the incorrect statement(s).

  1. Rapid formation and breaking of bubbles in the bulk of a liquid being heated is called evaporation.
  2. The spreading out and mixing of a substance with another substance due to motion of its particles is called diffusion.
  3. Petrol evaporates faster than water because interparticle forces are stronger in water.
  4. Ice-cream feels colder than ice cold water because of latent heat of fusion.
questions.explanation: Rapid formation and breaking of bubbles in the bulk of a liquid being heated is called boiling.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly represents the given substances in increasing order of kinetic energy of their particles?
questions.explanation: Kinetic energy (molecular motion) of the particles increases from solid to liquid to gas. Thus, the correct increasing order of kinetic energy of particles is brick, honey, water, air.

questions.question: An organic compound X has a melting point of -33oC and a boiling point of 66oC. Which of the following is true about this substance?
questions.explanation: Substance exists as a liquid between -33oC to 66oC (i.e., at 0oC). At -100oC, it will be a solid and at 90oC, it will be in gaseous state.

questions.question:
An experiment is performed as shown in the given figure.

The conclusion we can draw from the given experiment is that

  1. Nature of matter is continuous
  2. Matter is made up of particles
  3. Particles of salt get into the spaces between the particles of water
  4. Both Matter is made up of particles and Particles of salt get into the spaces between the particles of water
questions.explanation: When common salt is added to water, the particles of salt get into the empty spaces between the particles of water.

questions.question:
A brief information about three substances is given in the table.

Substance Melting point Boiling point
P 23oC 60oC
Q 10oC 20oC
R 65oC 110oC

Which of the following is incorrect about these substances?

options.option: At room temperature substance R will have strongest interparticle forces.
At room temperature, substance P will have fixed volume but no fixed shape.
At room temperature substance Q will have maximum space between the particles.

questions.explanation: Substance P exists in liquid state, Q exists in gaseous state while R exists in solid state at room temperature.

questions.question:
Choose the correct statements about the plasma state of matter.

  1. Plasma state consists of super energetic and super excited particles.
  2. The particles of plasma state are in the form of ionised gases.
  3. The plasma is created on stars.
  4. Colour of plasma glow depends on the temperature of gas only.
questions.explanation: The plasma glows with a special colour depending on the nature of the gas, not only on the temperature of the gas.

questions.question:
Observe the given figure carefully.

Which of the following statements is incorrect?

options.option: Water vapours formed during process II when come in contact with skin give out 22.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 105 J/kg more heat than the boiling water.
Latent heat for process I is 3.34 {tex}\times{/tex} 105 J/kg.
Processes I, II and III are endothermic while processes IV, V and VI are exothermic.

questions.explanation: None of these

questions.question:
The given apparatus is used to study the diffusion of a number of gases at the same temperature and pressure.

Which of the following pairs of gases would diffuse into the vacuum at the same speed?
[Given : Atomic mass of H = 1 u, C = 12 u, N = 14 u, S = 32 u, O = 16 u]
questions.explanation: Rate of diffusion depends upon the molecular mass of gases. CO and N2 have the same molecular mass hence, both will diffuse at the same rate.

questions.question:
Diagrams W, X and Y show how the particles of a substance are packed at different temperatures.

The given graph shows the temperature changes which occur on warming the substance.

In which region of the graph would all the particles be packed as in Y?
questions.explanation: III

questions.question:
Observe the given diagram showing changes in states of matter carefully.

Which of the following is correct identification for processes P to U?
questions.explanation: P - Fusion, R - Condensation, T - Sublimation

questions.question:
Match column I with column II and select the correct option from the codes given below.

Column I Column II
(a) Liquid (i) Highly compressible
(b) Gas (ii) Definite volume
(c) Plasma (iii) Super low density
(d) Bose-Einstein condensate (iv) Super energetic
Which of the following solution scatter light?
questions.explanation: As the particle size of both colloidal & suspension is large they are able to scatter light.

questions.question: Cooking of food and digestion of food:-

options.option: cooking is physical whereas digestion is chemical

questions.explanation: The cooking of food and digestion of food are both chemical process as in both chemical reaction takes place.

questions.question: Mercury and Bromine are both
questions.explanation: Mercury and Bromine are both liquids at room temperature as both have low melting points.

questions.question: Blood and seawater are:-

options.option: Blood is a compound and seawater is a mixture
Blood is a mixture whereas seawater is a compound

questions.explanation: Blood and seawater are both mixtures as blood contains many components like RBC and WBC and many more, as well as seawater also contain NaCl and other components as a mixture.

questions.question: Sol and Gel are examples of

options.option: Sol is a liquid-solid colloid and Gel is a liquid-liquid colloid
Sol is a solid-solid colloid and Gel is a solid-liquid colloid
Sol is a solid-liquid colloid and Gel is a liquid-solid colloid

questions.explanation: Sol is a solid-liquid colloid and Gel is a liquid-solid colloid

questions.question: Sugar dissolves in water to form a sugar solution. What is the name of the solute?
questions.explanation: Sugar dissolves in water to form a sugar solution. Here sugar is solute and water is solvent.

questions.question: Boron and carbon:-

options.option: Boron is metallic and Carbon is a metal
Boron is metalloid and Carbon is non-metal
boron is non-metal and carbon is a metalloid

questions.explanation: Metalloids are the elements that have properties intermediate between those of metals and nonmetals. E.g.- Boron. Whereas Carbon is a solid and is a nonmetal having properties of nonmetal.

questions.question: Dissolving sugar is an example of:
questions.explanation: We can get back sugar crystals by evaporating water.

questions.question: Rusting of an article made up of iron is called

options.option: corrosion and it is a physical as well as chemical change
dissolution and it is a chemical change
corrosion and it is a chemical change

questions.explanation: corrosion and it is a chemical change

questions.question: Which one is a physical change?
questions.explanation: Adding of common salt (NaCl) to water is physical change as no new substance is formed and no heat is evolved during the addition of salt in water. Also, salt can be obtained by evaporation.

questions.question: Which of the following are chemical changes?

options.option: Drying of wet clothes in sun light

questions.explanation: The cooking of vegetables is a chemical change because a new substance is formed having new properties and heat is used to change the state. The cooked vegetable cannot be reversed back as a raw vegetable.

questions.question:
What happens on adding dilute HCl to a mixture of iron filling and sulphur powder?

  1. H2S is formed.
  2. A colourless and odourless gas is formed.
  3. A greenish solution appears.
  4. FeS is formed.
questions.explanation:
Fe(s) + 2HCl(aq) → FeCl2(aq) + H2(g)
Sulfur will not react with HCl and it will not conduct electricity.
FeCl2 is a Pale blue-green. And H2 is a colourless and odourless gas.
So, Statement B and C are the correct statements.

questions.question: The substance which does not form a true solution in water is:
questions.explanation: Egg albumin has a particle size of the order 10-9 to 10-12 i.e. of the order of colloidal size. Due to which it is not able to form a clear solution in water and results into a colloidal solution.

questions.question: A change is said to be a physical change when
questions.explanation:
Some of the characteristics of a physical change are:

  1. Temporary in nature.
  2. No energy change occur.
  3. Does not affect the internal structure of a substance, only the molecules are rearranged.
  4. No new substance is formed.
So all statements are correct.

questions.question: The components of the compound can be separated by using:

options.option: can be separated by using any method.
cannot be separated by using any method.

questions.explanation: The components of the compound can be separated only by chemical methods because they have undergone chemical changes while formation which is physically irreversible in nature.

questions.question: A student added only two drops of iodine to a rice extract in test tube 'A'. Another student added a little rice extract to iodine solution in test tube 'B'. They would then observe:

options.option: a change of colour to blue-black in both tubes 'A' and 'B'
no change of colour in any test tube
a change of colour to blue-black in test tube 'A' but not in test tube 'B'
a change of colour to blue-black in test tube 'B' but not in test tube 'A'

questions.explanation:
Iodine + Starch Solution {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Blue-black colour
Starch act as an indicator of the presence of iodine.

questions.question: Fermentation of grapes is an example of
questions.explanation:
1. The new substance is formed
  2. It is not reversible
  3. It is permanent
  4. After fermentation, you are not able to obtain grapes again.
    So, Fermentation of grapes is an example of chemical change.

questions.question: Metanil yellow, an adulterant used in Arhar dal is basically:
questions.explanation: Metanil yellow is a yellow colour dye, generally used colouring food material, for e.g. starch when dyed with metanil yellow looks like turmeric. Poor quality dal when dyed with metanil yellow looks superior quality dal.

questions.question: To observe starch granules in potato under a microscope, freshly cut surface of potato placed on a slide. The stain that will show starch granules clearly is:
questions.explanation: Place a slice of a potato on a paper towel at top of a paper plate and add a drop of the iodine solution to the potato slice. Note the color change. A change of color to blue-black or purple color suggests that starch is present. If there is no change in color, this suggests no starch is detectable.

questions.question: A food sample turned blue-black after addition of a few drops of iodine solution. This sample contains:
questions.explanation: Many different food groups contain a carbohydrate known as starch. Using an iodine solution, you can test for the presence of starch. When starch is present, the iodine changes from brown to blue-black or purple.

questions.question: Which one of the following will form a translucent solution in water?
questions.explanation: Starch forms a colloidal solution. Colloidal solutions are translucent and their particles can pass through filter paper to give a translucent filtrate.

questions.question: Which will not give a stable solution even when stirred for sometimes?
questions.explanation: Egg albumin in water will not give a stable solution. The protein in egg albumin coagulated to form a lump in hot water.

questions.question: The system when starch is added to hot water is:
questions.explanation: The colloid of starch is prepared by the dispersion method. 2-3 g of powdered/crushed starch is dissolved in 3- 4 ml of water to make a thin paste. This paste is added to100 ml of boiling water while stirring. Allow the solution to cool and filter. The filtrate is colloid of starch.

questions.question: Which type of solution is formed when sand and water are mixed thoroughly and then kept undisturbed for some time?
questions.explanation: Sand will form a suspension in water. If you shake a container of sand and water, the sand spreads through the water, forming a cloudy liquid. The sand will then settle to the bottom of the container as sediment. Suspensions often need to be shaken or stirred before use to spread the sediment through the liquid.

questions.question: Which of the following settles down when allowed to stand undisturbed doe sometimes?
questions.explanation: Muddy water will settle down because particles are heavy and settle due to gravity. Setting down of coarse particles under the influence of gravity is called sedimentation. During sedimentation, heavier particles settle down faster than finer particles.

questions.question: Tyndall effect is observed in which one of the following?
questions.explanation: Starch forms a colloid in water (hot water).

questions.question: A student mixed the white of an egg with water and stirred it well. After sometimes he observe that:

options.option: egg white floats on the surface of the water
egg white settles down at the bottom

questions.explanation: Egg white is translucent.  Egg albumin forms colloidal solution which is translucent.

questions.question: Choose the correct observation about a solution of sugar in water prepared by you in the laboratory.

options.option: Its particles are seen by naked eyes
Its components can be separated by filtration
Solid particles settle at the bottom after sometimes

questions.explanation: A solution is always transparent, light passes through it without scattering as the solute particles are very small in size. So, the solution of sugar in water is transparent.

questions.question: The particles of the colloidal solution are:
questions.explanation: The size of particles of the colloidal solution lies between 10-7 cm to 10-4 cm in diameter. So, The particles of colloidal solutions are visible with a powerful microscope.

questions.question: To prepare a colloidal solution of starch, we should:

options.option: heat starch, add it to cold water and then bring it to boil
add starch powder to cold water and boil
add the starch powder to boiling water and cool
add the thin paste of starch to hot water with stirring

questions.explanation: The colloid of starch is prepared by the dispersion method. 2-3 g of powdered/crushed starch is dissolved in 3-4 ml of water to make a thin paste. This paste is added to100 ml of boiling water while stirring. Allow the solution to cool and filter. The filtrate is colloid of starch.

questions.question: To prepare iron sulphide, by heating a mixture of iron filings and sulphur powder, we should use a:
questions.explanation: China's dish is the right apparatus used for strong heating. So, china's dish is used for heating as it has a high melting point and does not react with sulphur.

questions.question: Which one of the following will result in the formation of a mixture?

options.option: Agitating a detergent with water in a washing machine
Breaking of ice cubes into small pieces
Crushing of a marble tile into small particles

questions.explanation: Detergent in water will form a mixture, others will not form a mixture.

questions.question: A mixture of sulphur and carbon disulphide is

options.option: homogeneous and does not show Tyndall effect
heterogeneous and does not show Tyndall effect

questions.explanation:
Sulphur and carbon disulphide do not form a uniform composition and the properties of the mixture are not same throughout.
Therefore, it forms a heterogeneous composition.
Moreover, it shows a Tyndall effect, because in water sulphur remains suspended whereas carbon disulphide settles down as a layer at the bottom.

questions.question: Tincture of iodine has antiseptic properties. This solution is made by dissolving
questions.explanation: Tincture of iodine solution is made by dissolving iodine in alcohol. It contains around 2 - 7% iodine dissolved in a mixture of ethanol and water.

questions.question:
Which of the following are homogeneous in nature

  1. ice
  2. wood
  3. soil
  4. air
questions.explanation:
A mixture is said to be a homogeneous mixture if its constituents are distributed uniformly and are not physically distinct. Wood and soil are heterogeneous mixtures. Ice is made up of water and water is a pure compound. Air is a mixture of various gases.
Ice and air are homogeneous in nature.

questions.question:
Which of the following are physical changes?

  1. Melting of iron metal
  2. Rusting of iron
  3. Bending of an iron rod
  4. Drawing a wire of iron metal
questions.explanation: Melting of iron metal, bending of an iron rod and drawing wire of iron metal are all physical changes whereas rusting of iron is a chemical change.

questions.question: Arun has prepared 0.01% (by mass) solution of sodium chloride in water. Which of the following correctly represents the composition of the solutions?
questions.explanation:
Mass% = {tex}\frac{\text { mass of solute } }{ \text { (mass of solute }+\text { mass of solvent) } }\times 100{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{0.01 g \times 100}{(0.01+99.99) 0}=\frac{0.01 \times 100}{100.00}=0.01 \%{/tex}
Hence, the mass of solute =0.01 g  and the mass of solvent = 99.99gms.

questions.question:
Which of the following are physical changes?

  1. Decaying of wood
  2. Burning of wood
  3. Sawing of wood
  4. Hammering of a nail into a piece of wood
questions.explanation:
Option (i) Decaying of wood and (ii) Burning of wood are chemical changes because in these processes, the chemical composition of wood is changed and new substances are formed. Which cannot be converted back into their original form.
In (iii) Sawing of wood and (iv) Hammering of a nail into a piece of wood, the chemical composition of wood is not changed, therefore, (iii) and (iv) are physical changes.

subtopics.name: What are the Types of Pure Substances?

questions.question:
Neha took two China dishes and marked them as I and II.

In China dish I, she mixed iron filings and sulphur powder. In China dish II, she mixed iron filings and dilute hydrochloric acid. Which of the following statements is correct regarding this activity?

options.option: In China dish II, a new compound is formed, which is different from iron filings and dilute hydrochloric acid.
In China dish I, a mixture is formed, where iron filings and sulphur powder can be seen separately.
A colourless and odourless gas comes out from China dish II.

questions.explanation: In China dish I, iron filings and sulphur powder are mixed without heating so, no new compound is formed and both these substances can be seen separately. In China dish II, iron filings react with dilute hydrochloric acid to form a new compound (ferrous chloride) and a colourless, odourless gas (hydrogen) is evolved.

questions.question:
Boiling points of a few gases are given below:

Gas W X Y Z
Boiling point (oC) -152 -246 -196 -183

If liquid mixture of these gases is fractionally distilled, the order of gases distilling out from first to last is
questions.explanation: The gas having lowest boiling point i.e., highly volatile will be distilled out first and the gas having highest boiling point i.e., least volatile will be distilled out at the last. So, the correct order of gases distilling out is X, Y, Z, W.

questions.question:
A food sample was tested for the presence of components P, G, R and S and the following chromatogram was obtained.

The components not present in food sample are
questions.explanation: Chromatogram of food sample does not match with the chromatograms of components Q and S.

questions.question: Which of the following solutions has the highest mass by mass percentage?

options.option: 60 g of potassium permanganate in 200 g of water
10 g of sodium chloride in 200 g of water
20 g of sodium carbonate in 90 g of water
15 g of sugar in 160 g of water

questions.explanation:
Mass by mass percentage = {tex}\frac{\text { Mass of solute }}{\text { Mass of solution }} {/tex}  {tex}\times{/tex} 100
Concentration of solution I = {tex}\frac{10}{10+200}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100 = 4.76%
Concentration of solution II = {tex}\frac{15}{160+15}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100 = 8.57%
Concentration of solution III = {tex}\frac{60}{200+60}{/tex}  {tex}\times{/tex} 100 = 23.07%
Concentration of solution IV = {tex}\frac{20}{90+20}{/tex}  {tex}\times{/tex} 100 = 18.18%

questions.question:
Select the correct statement(s).

  1. A solution in which size of the solute particles is about 10-10 m, is called true solution.
  2. A solution which contains maximum possible amount of solute at any given temperature is called a saturated solution.
  3. In suspension, the size of particles is of the order of 10-7 m or larger.
  4. A colloid is a heterogeneous system.
questions.explanation: I, II, III and IV

questions.question: Symbol of Iron is:-
questions.explanation: Symbol "Fe" for Iron has been derived from the Latin word Ferrum.

questions.question: Atomicity of Chlorine and Argon is:
questions.explanation: Atomicity is the total number of atoms present in one molecule of an element or a compound. Chlorine molecule (Cl2) is diatomic i.e. a molecule of chlorine contains 2 atoms of chlorine. Argon is an inert gas and an atom of Argon is capable of independent existence. Hence, Argon is monoatomic (Word "mono" refers to the single atom).

questions.question: The Molecular mass of water (H2O) is:
questions.explanation:
The molecular mass of H2O is calculated as the sum of the atomic masses of Hydrogen and Oxygen multiplied by the respective number of their atoms.
Therefore, the molecular mass of water (H2O) = 2 {tex}\times {/tex} Atomic mass of Hydrogen (H) + Atomic mass of Oxygen (O)
The molecular mass of water = (2 {tex}\times {/tex} 1 g) + 16 g
mass of water = 18 g.

questions.question: Atomic radius is measured in nanometers and:
questions.explanation:
Metre is a bigger unit (and the SI unit) of length. A nanometer is a smaller unit of length. Prefixes and suffixes are used before the unit to represent smaller and bigger units. Nano means 10-9 times. 
Therefore,1 nm = 10-9 m.

questions.question: Oxygen is:
questions.explanation: Oxygen (with atomic number Z = 8) is a bivalent element. An atom of oxygen has 2 electrons in the innermost shell and 6 electrons in its outermost shell. It can accept 2 more electrons and complete an octet (8 electrons in the outermost shell).

questions.question: The molecular formula for Aluminum Chloride is:
questions.explanation: The correct molecular formula for Aluminum Chloride is AlCl3. Valency of Aluminium is +3 and that of Chlorine is -1. A chemical compound is always electrically neutral.

questions.question: Atomicity of fluorine is:
questions.explanation: The atomicity of an element is the number of atoms present in one molecule of the element. Atomicity of fluorine (F) will be the number of atoms present in one molecule of fluorine (F2).

questions.question: The molecular formula for calcium fluoride is:
questions.explanation: The molecular formula for calcium fluoride is CaF2. Valency of calcium is +2 and that of fluorine is -1.

questions.question: Nitrogen is:
questions.explanation: Nitrogen (N2) is diatomic. A nitrogen atom has 5 electrons in its outer shell. It forms three covalent bonds with another nitrogen atom and makes a nitrogen molecule.

questions.question: What is the molecular formula for Calcium Hydroxide?
questions.explanation: The molecular formula for Calcium Hydroxide is Ca(OH)2. The charge on calcium ion is 2+. Charge on [OH] radical is -1.

questions.question: The chemical formula of lead sulphate is
questions.explanation: PbSO4

questions.question: Calculate the formula unit mass of ZnCl2? (nearest approximation)
questions.explanation:
Formula unit mass of ZnCl2 is (66 + 35.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 137u) 
The atomic mass of Zn is 66 and the atomic mass of Cl is 35.5

questions.question: The sample of water from a well is analysed. What will be the ratio of hydrogen and oxygen in it by mass?
questions.explanation: The atomic mass of Hydrogen is 1 u and that of Oxygen is 16 u. The formula of water is H2O. Hence, the ratio of Hydrogen and Oxygen by mass in water will be 2:16 or 1:8. The ratio will be the same irrespective of the source of the water.

questions.question:
What information do we get from the molecular formula?

  1. It represents one molecule of the substance.
  2. It does not tell the name of the substance.
  3. It tells about the type of atoms.
  4. It represents the formula mass unit of the substance.
questions.explanation: The molecular formula of a substance (an element or a compound) is a symbolic representation of the actual number of atoms present in one molecule of that substance. It represents the formula mass unit of the substance. It also conveys the name of the substance. Therefore, (a), (c) and (d) are correct.

questions.question:
Which of the following elements are present in Quick lime?

  1. Calcium, Oxygen
  2. Sodium, Hydrogen, Oxygen
  3. Calcium, Bromine
  4. Calcium chloride
questions.explanation: The chemical formula of the Quick lime is CaO. So, Calcium (Ca) and Oxygen (O) elements are present in Quick lime.

questions.question: What is the formula of calcium phosphide?
questions.explanation: The valency of Calcium is 2 (Ca2+)and the valency of the Phosphide ion is 3 (P3-). A chemical compound is always electrically neutral; so the positive and negative valencies of the ions or radicals present in calcium phosphide should add up to zero. Therefore, the chemical formula of calcium phosphide is Ca3P2.

questions.question: A sample of CaCO3 contains 3.01 {tex}\times{/tex} 1023 ions of Ca+2 and {tex}{\text{C}}{{\text{O}}_{\text{3}}}^{ - {\text{2}}}{/tex}. The mass of the sample is:
questions.explanation: The mass of one mole of CaCO3 is equal to 100 g. 6.022 {tex}\times{/tex} 1023 ions are equivalent to one mole. Therefore, mass of 3.01 {tex}\times{/tex} 1023 ions will be equivalent to ({tex}100 \over 2{/tex}) g or 50 g.

questions.question: What mass of CO2 will 3.011 {tex}\times{/tex} 1023 molecules contain?
questions.explanation:
The molecular mass of CO2 is 44 (12 + 26 {tex}\times{/tex} 2).
6.022 {tex}\times{/tex} 1023 molecules of carbon will contain 44 g.
Hence, 3.011 {tex}\times{/tex} 1023 molecules of CO2 will contain a mass of 22 g.

questions.question: The number of molecules in CuSO4.5H2O bonded by H-bond is
questions.explanation: One molecule of water is linked by H-bond because only one molecule of water is present outside the coordination sphere.

questions.question: The formula for quicklime is
questions.explanation:
The formula for quick lime is CaO.
The Thermal decomposition of calcium carbonate gives calcium oxide which is also called as quick lime along with carbon dioxide.
CaCO3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CaO + CO2

questions.question: The chemical symbol for nitrogen gas is
questions.explanation:
• Nitrogen exists as nitrogen gas. It is a diatomic molecule. Therefore, it exists as N2​. The chemical symbol for the element nitrogen is N. 
  • Ni is the chemical symbol for a nickel. 
  • N+ is for nitrogen ion.

questions.question: The chemical symbol for sodium is
questions.explanation: Sodium is a chemical element with the symbol Na (from Latin natrium) and the atomic number is 11.

questions.question: A change in the physical state can be brought about

options.option: when energy is either given to, or taken out from the system
only when energy is given to the system
only when energy is taken out from the system

questions.explanation: A change in the physical state can be brought about when energy is either given to or taken out of the system. It is because energy change helps in changing the magnitude of attraction forces between the particles, thus helps in changing the physical states (j.e., solid, liquid, gas) of matter.

questions.question: Which of the following represents a correct chemical formula? Name it.
questions.explanation: BiPO4 is the correct formula, its name is Bismuth phosphate.

questions.question: What are a and b?

options.option: a = multiply by 22.4 L, b = multiply by 22.4 L
a = divide by 22.4 L, b = divide by 22.4 L
a = divide by 22.4 L, b - multiply by 22.4 L
a = multiply by 22.4 L, b = divide by 22.4 L

questions.explanation:
Volume of gas at STP = No. of moles {tex}\underbrace{\times 22.4 \mathrm{~L}}_a{/tex}
No. of moles = Volume of gas at STP {tex}\underbrace{\div 22.4 \mathrm{~L}}_b{/tex}

questions.question: An element P forms an oxide with formula PO. The formulae of its sulphate and phosphate will be respectively
questions.explanation:
Formula of oxide = PO
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Valency of P = +2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Formula of sulphate = PSO4
and phosphate = P3(PO4)2

questions.question:
Which of the following statements are incorrect?

  1. Dichromate ion is a divalent and positive ion.
  2. Barium ion is trivalent and positive.
  3. Solid sulphur is a polyatomic molecule.
  4. Ammonium ion is divalent and positive.
questions.explanation:
Dichromate ion - Cr2O72-
Barium ion - Ba2+, Ammonium ion - {tex}\mathrm{NH}_4^{+}{/tex}

questions.question: An ionic compound of element M and chlorine has the formula, MCl3. The molecular mass of MCl3 is 118.5. What will be the molecular mass of the oxide of element M?
questions.explanation:
The valency of M in MCl3 is +3 hence, the formula of the oxide of element M is M2O3.
Mass of element M in MCl3 = 118.5 - 3(35.5) = 12
Molecular mass of M2O3 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 12) + (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 16) = 72

questions.question:
The maximum amount of sodium that can be obtained by electrolysis of 117 g of sodium chloride is
[Atomic mass of Na = 23 u, Cl = 35.5 u]
questions.explanation:
Electrolysis decomposes ionic compounds into their elements.
Mass of NaCI = 23 + 35.5 = 58.5 u
Now, 58.5 g of NaCI contains 23 g of Na
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 117 g of NaCI will contain {tex}\frac{23}{58.5} \times 117{/tex} = 46 g of Na

questions.question:
Four different experiments were conducted in the following ways:

  1. 3 g of carbon was burnt in 8 g of oxygen to give 11 g of CO2.
  2. 1.2 g of carbon was burnt in air to give 4.2 g of CO2.
  3. 4.5 g of carbon was burnt in enough air to give 11 g of CO2.
  4. 4 g of carbon was burnt in oxygen to form 30.3 g of CO2.

Law of constant proportions is not illustrated in experiment(s).
questions.explanation:
According to law of constant proportions, the ratio of carbon and oxygen by weight should always be 3 : 8 in CO2.
In experiment i - C : O = 3 : 8
In experiment ii - C : O = 1.2 : 3
In experiment iii - C : O = 4.5 : 6.5
In experiment iv - C : O = 4 : 26.3

questions.question: Two samples X and Y of a pure substance obtained by two different methods contain elements P and Q. Substance X has a mass of 2.25 g and contains 0.90 g of Q. Substance Y is made up of 60% P and 40% Q by weight. This is an illustration of
questions.explanation:
Mass of X = 2.25 g
Mass of Q = 0.90 g
Thus, mass of P = 2.25 - 0.90 = 1.35 g
Ratio of masses of P and Q in sample ‘X' = 1.35 : 0.90 i.e., 1.5 : 1
Ratio of masses of P and Q in sample ‘Y’ = 60 : 40 i.e., 1.5 : 1
As the ratio of the masses of P and Q in two samples ‘X' and 'Y' is same i.e., 1.5 : 1 so, this is an illustration of ‘law of constant proportions’.

questions.question: Sulphate of a divalent metal M exists in hydrated form. If 0.10 mol of metal sulphate combines with 9.0 g of water to form the hydrated salt then, the formula of metal sulphate will be
questions.explanation:
As the metal is divalent, the formula of hydrated sulphate will be MSO4{tex}\cdot{/tex}xH2O 
To find the value of x:
0.10 mol of MSO4 combines with 9 g of H2O (i.e., 0.5 mol of water).
[{tex}\because{/tex} moles of H2O = {tex}\frac 9{18}{/tex} = 0.5]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 1 mol of MSO4 will combine with {tex}\frac{0.5}{0.10}{/tex} = 5 mol of water.
So, formula of sulphate is MSO4{tex}\cdot{/tex}5H2O.

questions.question: A neutron is:
questions.explanation:
A neutron is a neutral sub-atomic particle and has mass.
Mass of neutron is 1.0086654 u or 1.6749 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-27 kg.

questions.question: {tex}\beta {/tex} -particles are represented as:-
questions.explanation: {tex}{}_{ - 1}^0e{/tex} is the correct representation of a beta particle. A beta particle is denoted by the lower-case Greek letter beta (Symbol {tex}\beta{/tex}). It is a high energy, high-speed electron or positron emitted in the radioactive decay of a nucleus. It is sometimes called a beta ray. An electron has negligible mass - hence the superscript 0. An electron has a negative charge - hence a subscript -1.

questions.question: {tex}{}_{18}^{40}Ar{/tex} and {tex}{}_{20}^{40}Ca{/tex} are:
questions.explanation: Isobars. They have the same mass number (i.e. the same number of nucleons) but different atomic number (i.e. different number of protons).

questions.question: The maximum number of electrons in the L shell is:
questions.explanation: L shell can accommodate a maximum of 8 electrons.

questions.question: An atomic number of an element is equal to:
questions.explanation: Atomic number (Z) of a chemical element is the number of protons in the nucleus of that atom. It is a characteristic of the element and determines its place in the periodic table. Atomic number = number of protons.

questions.question: The charge of a proton (p+) is:
questions.explanation: A proton is a positively charged sub-atomic particle. The charge on a proton is +1.6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-19 C.

questions.question: {tex}_{10}^{20}Ne{/tex} and {tex}_{10}^{22}Ne{/tex} are:
questions.explanation: {tex}_{10}^{20}Ne{/tex} and {tex}_{10}^{22}Ne{/tex} have the same atomic number (Z = 10) but different mass numbers. They are isotopes (Atoms of the same element - Neon - with the same atomic number but a different mass number).

questions.question: Helium {tex}\left( {_2^4He} \right){/tex} has :
questions.explanation: Helium {tex}\left( {_2^4He} \right){/tex} has 2 protons (p+) and 2 neutrons (no). Mass number (A) = 4. Atomic number (Z) = number of protons = 2. Number of neutrons = A - Z = 4 - 2 = 2

questions.question: In which form is oxygen stable?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is stable as O2-. Oxygen is a non-metal and has an electronegative character. Oxygen molecules are diatomic.

questions.question: How many electrons will Na+ have in its outermost shell?
questions.explanation: Sodium (Na) has an atomic number of 11 (Configuration 2, 8, 1) and 1 electron in its outermost shell. It loses 1 electron easily to form Na+ ion. Na+ ion will have 8 electrons in its outermost shell.

questions.question: The atomic number of an element during a chemical reaction:
questions.explanation: Atomic number (number of protons) of an element remains constant during a chemical reaction.

questions.question: Electronic configuration of calcium is:
questions.explanation: The atomic number of calcium is 20. Number of electrons = number of protons = 20. Therefore, the electronic configuration of calcium is 2, 8, 8, 2.

questions.question: Neutron has:
questions.explanation: A neutron has mass but it has no charge.

questions.question: Rutherford's alpha-particle scattering experiment was responsible for the discovery of:
questions.explanation: Rutherford's alpha-particle scattering experiment was responsible for the discovery of the atomic nucleus. It was discovered in 1911 by Ernest Rutherford based on the 1909 Geiger–Marsden gold foil experiment.

questions.question: Isotopes of an element have:
questions.explanation: Isotopes of an element are atoms with the same number of protons but a different number of neutrons. Since isotopes of an element have the same atomic number, they show the same physical properties and chemical properties.

questions.question: Number of valence electrons in Cl– ion is:
questions.explanation: Atomic number of Chlorine is 17 (Configuration 2, 8, 7). Since the atom gains an electron during the formation of Cl– ion, the number of valence electrons in Cl– ion is 8.

questions.question:
{tex}{}_{88}^{226}A,{\rm{ }}{}_{87}^{228}B,{\rm{ }}{}_{88}^{228}C,{\rm{ }}{}_{90}^{228}D{/tex}
Which of the following nuclei are isotopes?
questions.explanation: Isotopes are the elements with the same atomic number but different mass numbers. The elements A and C are having the same atomic number 88 but different mass number 226 and 228 respectively.

questions.question: The mass number of an isotope of an element is 295, if its nucleus has 196 neutrons. What is its atomic number?
questions.explanation: Atomic number is the number of protons present in the given element and can be calculated by subtracting neutrons from mass number.

questions.question:
Isotopes of an element have:

  1. Same chemical properties
  2. Same atomic masses
  3. Different atomic numbers
  4. Atoms of the same element
questions.explanation: Isotopes are atoms with the same number of protons but a different number of neutrons. Since the atomic number is equal to the number of protons and the atomic mass is the sum of the number of protons and neutrons, it can also be said that isotopes are atoms of the same element with the same atomic number but a different mass number. They have the same chemical properties.

questions.question: How many electrons are present in the species He2+ ion?
questions.explanation: Helium (He) has two electrons in its atom. The cation (He2+) is formed through the loss of 2 electrons from the outer-most shell of the atom. Hence, the number of electrons in He2+ will be 0.

questions.question: In a species, the number of electrons is more than the number of protons. Predict its nature.
questions.explanation: An anion is formed by gaining electron(s) from other atoms. Hence, an anion contains more electrons than protons. On the other hand, a cation is formed through the loss of an electron(s). A neutral atom will have the same number of negatively-charged electrons as well as positively-charged protons.

questions.question: The order of the energy shells is:
questions.explanation: The energy of the shells increases from the inner-most to the outer-most shell. So the correct order of the energy of energy shells is K < L < M < N < O.

subtopics.name: How are Electrons Distributed In Different Orbits (Shells)?

questions.question: Out of electron, positron, alpha particle and proton, which particle is regarded as the universal particle?
questions.explanation: Electron is considered as a universal particle as it is present in all atoms. It also provides the chemical properties to the atoms.

questions.question:
In K+ and K-:

  1. Number of electrons is the same
  2. Number of protons is the same
  3. Number of neutrons are the same
  4. Number of shells are the same
questions.explanation: Different ions are formed by an atom through the gain or loss of electrons. Hence, the number of protons and neutrons remains the same.

questions.question: The {tex}\frac{charge}{mass}{/tex} ratio of electron

options.option: depends upon both nature of gas and nature of electrodes

questions.explanation: The {tex}\frac{charge}{mass}{/tex} on the type of ray one is passing. Since we are talking about electron the cathode ray particles are nothing but a beam of electrons. Since the charge, as well as mass, is constant for electron no matter any gas we take in during the experiment in the discharge tube. So, their charge/mass ratio remains constant for electron.

questions.question: Rutherford's scattering experiment fails for very small scattering angles because

options.option: the impact parameter between the {tex}\alpha{/tex} - particle source and the nucleus of the target is very large as compared to the size of the nucleus.
whole nuclear charge of the target atom is not screened by its electrons at all.

questions.explanation:
1. An atom consists of a minute positively charged body at its center called a nucleus. The nucleus contains all the protons and neutrons. Since the total mass of the atom is because of protons and neutrons, hence the entire mass located at the centre of the atom. Hence few alpha particles deflected due to positive charge of the nucleus.
  2. An atom consists of a sufficient number of extremely small negatively charged electrons distributed around the nucleus to balance the positive charge of the nucleus. Hence the maximum volume of an atom remains empty so many alpha particles moved without any deflection. Since a very less number of alpha particles deflected, hence the volume of nucleus is very less compared to the whole atom. The size of nucleus is less than 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-14 m while the size of an atom is around 10-10 m.

questions.question: 1 mole of diatomic element X2 contains 34 and 40 moles of electrons and neutrons respectively. The isotopic formula of the element is
questions.explanation:
Since it contains 34 moles of electrons the atomic number is 17. The molecule contains 40 moles of neutrons hence it has 20 neutrons per atom. The formula for the element will be 17X37.
Elements having the same atomic number but different atomic masses are known as Isotopes. So, 17X40 is isotope of 17X37.

questions.question: The conclusion of Rutherford's scattering experiment does not include

options.option: Scattering follows coulomb's law, i.e., same charges repel each other.
{tex}\alpha{/tex}-particles can come within the distance of order of 10-14 m of the nucleus.
The positively charged particles of atom move with very high velocity.
The radius of nucleus is less than 10-14 m.

questions.explanation:
Conclusion of Rutherford's scattering experiment:

  1. Most of the space inside the atom is empty because most of the {tex}\alpha{/tex}-particles passed through the gold foil without getting deflected.
  2. Very few particles were deflected from their path, indicating that the positive charge of the atom occupies very little space.
  3. A very small fraction of {tex}\alpha{/tex}-particles were deflected by very large angles, indicating that all the positive charge and mass of the gold atom were concentrated in a very small volume within the atom.
  4. Since a very less number of alpha particles deflected, hence the volume of the nucleus is very less compared to the whole atom. The size of the nucleus is less than 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-14 m while the size of an atom is around 10-10 m.
    So, the positively charged particles of atom move with very high velocity do not include the conclusion of Rutherford's scattering experiment.

questions.question: A student weights 30 kg. Suppose his body is entirely made up of electrons. How many electrons are there in his body?
questions.explanation:
Mass of electron = 9.1 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-31 kg.
Number of electron in the body of student = {tex}\frac{total\;mass}{mass\;of\;each\;electrons}\;=\;\frac{30kg}{9.1\;\times\;10^{-31}\;kg}{/tex} = 3.29 {tex}\times{/tex} 1031
Therefore, the student is made up of approximately 3.29 {tex}\times{/tex} 1031 electrons.

questions.question: An element has 3 valence electrons in its outermost 3rd shell, the name of element is
questions.explanation:
Number of electrons = 2 + 8 + 3 = 13.
The atomic number 13 belongs to the element Aluminum (Al).

questions.question: The electronic configuration of Cl– ion is
questions.explanation:
Neutral Chlorine (17Cl) has an electronic configuration as 2, 8,7.
Now we observe that Chlorine is one electron less than achieving the stable electronic configuration of its nearest Noble gas, Argon. So Chloride gains (steals) an electron (not from empty space, either from metal, semi-metal etc.) and becomes Chloride ion (so it becomes stable like Argon). So the electronic configuration of Chloride ion will be 2, 8, 8.

questions.question: Isotopes of an element have
questions.explanation: Elements having same atomic number but different atomic masses are known as Isotopes. Isotope have the same number of protons (and electrons), but different numbers of neutrons.

questions.question:
Which of the following are isotopes and which are isobars?
Argon (Ar), Deuterium (D), Calcium (Ca), Tritium (T), Protium (P)

options.option: Ar, Ca are isobars and D, T, P are isotopes
Ar, Ca are isotopes and D, T, P are isobars

questions.explanation:
Elements having the same atomic number but different atomic masses are known as Isotopes.
Protium(Hydrogen) -1, Deuterium – 2, Tritium -3 are three isotopes of hydrogen.
Atoms having the same atomic mass and different atomic numbers are known as Isobars. Example Argon, Calcium.

questions.question: The triad which is isoelectronic
questions.explanation:
An Isoelectronic series is a group of atoms/ions that have the same number of electrons.
Examples: N3-, O2-, F-, Ne, Na+, Mg2+, Al3+
This series each have 10 electrons.
P3-, S2-, Cl-, Ar, K+, Ca2+, Sc3+
This series each have 18 electrons.

questions.question: An element has electronic configuration 2, 8, 4. It will be classified as
questions.explanation:
There are some elements which have properties of metals and non-metal, such elements are known as Metalloids. Si have electronic configuration of 2, 8, 4.
Examples of Metalloids: Boron (B), Silicon (Si), Germanium (Ge), Arsenic (As), Antimony (Sb), Tellurium (Te), Polonium (Po), Astatine (At) etc.

questions.question: The isotope of carbon which has same number of neutrons as 8O16, is used in radiocarbon dating to determine age of old samples of living organisms?
questions.explanation: Carbon-14, 6C14, or radiocarbon, is a radioactive isotope of carbon with an atomic nucleus containing 6 protons and 8 neutrons. Its presence in organic materials is the basis of the radiocarbon dating method.

questions.question: Cathode rays have
questions.explanation: A cathode ray is a beam of electrons and electrons have mass. When cathode rays are passed through an electric field created by applying a potential across the plates P1 and P2, it is found that the cathode rays particles get deflected towards the positive plate. This indicates that the cathode rays particles carry a negative charge on them. So, Cathode rays have a charge as well as mass.

questions.question: The isotopes of hydrogen which contain same number of electrons, protons and neutrons?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen element has three isotopes: protium, deuterium, and tritium having the same atomic number of 1 but different mass number 1, 2, 3 respectively. Deuterium is isotopes of hydrogen which contain the same number of electrons, protons, and neutrons.

questions.question: Which of the following has the highest n/p ratio?
questions.explanation:
Number of neutron of {tex} {}_1^3H\; {/tex}= 3 – 1 = 2
Number of proton of {tex} {}_1^3H\; {/tex} = 1
N/p ratio {tex} {}_1^3H\; {/tex} = {tex}\begin{array}{l}\frac21\\\end{array}{/tex} = 2
N/P ratio of  {tex}{}_{92}^{235}U\;{/tex} = 1.55
N/P ratio of  {tex}{}_6^{14}C{/tex} = 1.33
N/p ratio of  {tex}{}_{88}^{222}Ra{/tex} = 1.52
