questions.question: The number of valence electrons determines

options.option: Both Physical and chemical properties of elements
Neither physical nor chemical properties of elements

questions.explanation: The number and arrangement of electrons in the outermost shell, or valence shell, produce the chemical properties of an atom. Atoms with filled valence shells are stable. Examples of stable atoms are Helium (He) with 2 valence electrons in the first shell. Neon (Ne) with 8 valence electrons in the second shell. Atoms with less than eight valence electrons tend to lose, gain, or share electrons with other atoms to have 8 valence electrons and become stable. There are, of course, exceptions to this "Octet Rule". Na has 1 valence electron. Na loses this electron (or Na is oxidized) and becomes the stable cation Na+ with 8 valence electrons. Na+ is the most abundant cation in seawater and extracellular fluid.

questions.question: Atomic number is equal to

options.option: difference in mass number and number of electrons

questions.explanation: The number of protons in the nucleus of the atom is equal to the atomic number (Z). The number of electrons in a neutral atom is equal to the number of protons. The mass number of the atom (M) is equal to the sum of the number of protons and neutrons in the nucleus. The number of neutrons is equal to the difference between the mass number of the atom (M) and the atomic number (Z).

questions.question:
Fill in the gap using given analogy
Atomic number : Number of protons :: Mass number : _______.
questions.explanation:
Atomic number: The total number of protons in the nucleus of an atom gives us the atomic number of that atom. It is represented by the letter Z. All the atoms of a particular element have the same number of protons, and hence the same atomic number. Atoms of different elements have different atomic numbers.
Mass number: The number of protons and neutrons combined give us the mass number of an atom. It is represented using the letter ‘A.’ As both protons and neutrons are present in the nucleus of an atom, they are together called nucleons.

questions.question: The highest value of e/m of anode rays has been observed when the discharge tube is filled with
questions.explanation: The e/m ratio is charge by mass ratio. Anode rays are the positively charged nuclei of the element in the discharge tube. It is a general observation that as the molecular mass of the gas increases its e/m ratio decreases. In the case of anode rays maximum value for the e/m ratio is observed in the case of Hydrogen gas (H2). The reason is that Hydrogen is the only element that does not carry any neutron. In anode ray discharge it carries 1 positive charge and mass is also 1. So the e/m ratio is 1. But for other gases as the molecular mass of the gas increases, the number of neutrons also increases so charge(number of protons) by mass (number of proton + number of the neutron) does not remain 1 but it becomes less than because mass is always greater than charge except for hydrogen.

questions.question: When a gold sheet is bombarded by a beam of {tex}\alpha{/tex}-particles, only a few of them got deflected whereas most go straight, undeflected. This is because

options.option: the volume of nucleus is smaller than that of atom.
the force of attraction exerted on α- particles by electrons is insufficient.
the force of repulsion acting on fast moving α- particle is very small.

questions.explanation: A small fraction of {tex}\alpha{/tex}-particles were deflected through small angles and a few through larger angles. Some of the {tex}\alpha{/tex}-particles suffered deflection by 90 or even larger angels. For this to happen, {tex}\alpha{/tex}-particles (positively charged) must approach a heavy positively charged core inside the atom (like charges repel each other). This heavy positively charged core inside the atom was named as the nucleus. Since the number of α- particles that bounced back was very small, hence, the volume occupied by the nucleus is very small as compared to the total volume of the atom.

questions.question: Which of the following statements does not belong to Bohr's model?

options.option: The electron in the orbit nearest to the nucleus is in lowest energy state and farthest away from nucleus is in highest energy state.
Electrons revolve around the nucleus in different orbits having fixed energies.
The electrons radiate energy during revolution due to force of attraction between nucleus and electrons.
Energy of the electrons in the orbit is quantized, i.e., they have fixed energies.

questions.explanation:
Postulates of Bohr’s Model of an Atom:

  1. In an atom, electrons (negatively charged) revolve around the positively charged nucleus in a definite circular path called orbits or shells.
  2. Each orbit or shell has fixed energy and these circular orbits are known as orbital shells.
  3. The energy levels are represented by an integer (n = 1, 2, 3,…) known as a quantum number. This range of quantum numbers starts from the nucleus side with n = 1 having the lowest energy level. The orbits n = 1, 2, 3, 4, … are assigned as K, L, M, N, …. shells and when an electron attains the lowest energy level it is said to be in the ground state.
  4. The electrons in an atom move from lower energy level to higher energy level by gaining the required energy and an electron moves from a higher energy level to lower energy level by losing energy.
    The electrons radiate energy during revolution due to the force of attraction between nucleus and electrons this statement does not belong to Bohr's model.

questions.question: How many electrons, protons and neutrons will be present in X –, if atomic number of X is 9 and mass number is 19?
questions.explanation:
Atomic number = 9
Mass number = 19
No of electron = No. of neutrons = 19 – 9 = 10
No. of proton = 19 – 10 = 9

questions.question: Which of the following are isobars?
questions.explanation: Atoms having the same atomic mass and different atomic numbers are known as Isobars. {tex}_{18}^{40}Ar\;,\;_{20}^{40}Ca{/tex}. Both the elements have the same atomic mass equal to 40 but different atomic numbers, i.e. argon has an atomic number equal to 18 and calcium has an atomic number equal to 20.

questions.question: Rutherford's {tex}\alpha{/tex}- scattering experiment led to the conclusion that

options.option: the mass and the positive charge of an atom are concentrated in the nucleus.

questions.explanation:
The Rutherford's Model of an Atom is as follows:

  1. Most of the part in an atom is empty.
  2. There is a positively charged center in an atom, which contains nearly the whole mass of the atom. The center is called the nucleus.
  3. The size of the nucleus is very small compared to an atom.
  4. Electrons revolve around the nucleus.

questions.question: Which of the following reactions led to the discovery of the neutron?
questions.explanation:
In 1932 J. Chadwick discovered another subatomic particle called the neutron. Neutron is present in the nucleus of all atoms. And the reaction is given below.
{tex}{}_4^9Be\;\;+\;{}_2^4He\;\rightarrow{}_6^{12}C\;+{}_0^1n{/tex}

questions.question: Which of the following pairs have identical values of charge/mass?
questions.explanation: Deuterium and an {tex}\alpha{/tex} - alpha particle has identical e/m ratio. They both emit and absorb at the same frequencies. The e/m ratio of an alpha particle is 4.82245111 {tex}\times{/tex} 107 C/kg which is identical with deuterium.

questions.question: If K, L, M, N, shells of an atom are full. The total number of electrons in that atom are:
questions.explanation:
Maximum number of electrons in K-shell i.e. 1st shell = 2
Maximum number of electrons in L-shell = 8
Maximum number of electrons in M-shell = 18
Maximum number of electrons in N-shell = 32
2 + 8 + 18 + 32 = 60

questions.question: The difference between isotopes and isobars

options.option: Isotopes have same physical properties but isobars have different.
Isotopes have same chemical properties but isobars have different.
Isobars have same chemical properties but isotopes have different chemical proerties.

questions.explanation: Isotope compounds have the same number of electrons. Isotopes have the same chemical properties as the number of electrons present in them are the same because only electrons participate in chemical reactions. While isobar compounds have the same mass number but the different atomic numbers and chemical properties depend upon the number of electrons so isobars have different chemical properties.

questions.question: A has 9 protons, 9 electrons and 10 neutrons. B has 12 protons, 12 electrons and 12 neutrons. Formula between A and B is:
questions.explanation:
'A' has 9 protons, 9 electrons, and 10 neutrons. So, the valency of A is 7 means it can easily accept one electron.
'B' has 12 protons, 12 electrons, and 12 neutrons. So, valance of B is 2 means it can easily donate two electron.
So, Formula between A and B is BA2.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly represent the electronic distribution in the Mg atom?
questions.explanation:
Atomic number of magnesium = 12
Therefore number of electrons = 12
Thus, electronic configuration of magnesium is Magnesium = 2,8,2.

questions.question: The number of electrons in an element x is 15 and the number of neutrons is 16. Which of the following is the correct representation of the element ?
questions.explanation:
An element that contains 15 electrons is Phosphours(P).
Now mass of this element = No. of electrons = No. of neutrons = 15 = 16 = 31.
So, the representation of the element is {tex}{}_{15}^{31}X{/tex} .

questions.question: In a sample of ethyl ethanoate (CH3COOC2H5) the two oxygen atoms have the same number electrons but different number of neutrons. Which of the following is the correct reason for it ?

options.option: One of the oxygen atom has gained electrons
One of the oxygen atom has gained neutrons

questions.explanation:
The two oxygen atoms have the same number electrons but different number of neutrons. So, two oxygen atom have same atomic number but different atomic masses. Elements having same atomic number but different atomic masses are known as Isotopes.
So, the two oxygen atoms are isotopes is true statement.

questions.question: Elements with valency 1 are
questions.explanation: Elements with valency 1 are easily loss or gain electron. So, elements with valency 1 are either metals or non-metals. For example hydrogen is nonmetal and Lithium is metal both having valency 1.

questions.question: The first model of an atom was given by
questions.explanation: J.J Thomson was the first one to give atom's model. Which is also called plum pudding model/ watermelon model/ Raisin model as in his atom's structure he said that atom is a positively charged sphere with negative charge embedded in it like black seeds embedded in watermelon.

questions.question: An atom with 3 protons and 4 neutrons will have valency of
questions.explanation:
Atomic number = Number of protons = 3
Mass number = Number of protons + number of neutrons =3 + 4 = 7
Electronic configuration of the atom is 2,1(K,L)
Hence, its valency is 1.

questions.question: The electron distribution in an aluminium atom is
questions.explanation:
Atomic number of aluminium = 13.
Therefore number of electrons = 13.
Thus, electronic configuration of aluminium is (K = 2, L = 8, M = 3)

questions.question: The fundamental particle not present in hydrogen is ________.
questions.explanation: In nucleus of an atom, protons and neutrons are present. But in hydrogen atom, neutrons are not present, only 1 proton is present.

questions.question: In neutral atoms, number of electrons are equal to number of ________.
questions.explanation:
1. The number of protons in the nucleus of the atom is equal to the atomic number (Z).
  2. The number of electrons in a neutral atom is equal to the number of protons.
  3. The mass number of the atom (M) is equal to the sum of the number of protons and neutrons in the nucleus.
  4. The number of neutrons is equal to the difference between the mass number of the atom (M) and the atomic number (Z).

questions.question:
Dalton’s atomic theory successfully explained

  1. Law of conservation of mass
  2. Law of constant composition
  3. Law of radioactivity
  4. Law of multiple proportions
questions.explanation: Dalton's theory explained the law of conservation of mass, the law of constant composition, and the law of multiple proportions. Dalton's theory did not talk about any law of radioactivity.

questions.question: The ion of an element has 3 positive charges. Mass number of the atom is 27 and the number of neutrons is 14. What is the number of electrons in the ion?
questions.explanation:
Mass number (A) of the element = 27
Number of neutrons in the atom = 14
Hence, the number of electrons in the atom
= Mass number (A) - number of neutrons in the atom
= 27 - 14 = 13
Since the ion of the element has 3 positive charges, so the number of electrons in the ion is 13 - 3 = 10.
The number of electrons in the ion is 10.

questions.question: Which of the following represents the correct composition of the three isotopes of carbon?
questions.explanation:
Isotopes are the atoms of the same element, having same atomic number but different mass numbers. For a neutral atom,
Atomic number = Number of protons = Number of electrons
Mass number = Number of protons + Number of neutrons.
As the mass number of isotopes are 12, 13 and 14 so, number of neutrons will be
12 - 6 = 6, 13 - 6 = 7 and 14 - 6 = 8 in C - 12, C - 13 and C - 14 respectively.

questions.question:
A thin sheet of gold foil is bombarded with {tex}\alpha{/tex}-particles as in Rutherford’s experiment. Which of the given descriptions most accurately represents the path of {tex}\alpha{/tex}-particles?

  Undeflected No. of particles deflected through a small angle No. of particles deflected through a large angle
I All None None
II Most A few None
III Most A few A few
IV A few Most A few
questions.explanation: III option is correct as most of the alpha particles are undeflected while a few alpha particles are deflected through small and large angles.

questions.question: Which of the following schematic structures represent(s) cation(s)?
questions.explanation:
Structure I shows neutral atom having equal number of protons and electrons.
Structure II represents an anion which has more number of electrons than protons while structures III and IV represent cations having more number of protons than electrons.

questions.question:
There are two atomic species X and Y, such that

Atomic species X Y
Protons 8 8
Protons 8 10

Which of the following statements is true about X and Y?

options.option: X and Y have different physical properties.
X and Y have different chemical properties.

questions.explanation: X and Y have same number of protons but different number of neutrons i.e., same atomic number but different mass numbers hence, these are isotopes. Isotopes have same chemical properties but different physical properties.

questions.question:
A brief information about two atoms X and Y is given:
X: Atomic number = 7, Mass number = 14
Y: Atomic number = 7, Mass number = 15
Which of the following is correct about these two atoms?

options.option: Electronic configuration of X is 2, 8, 4 while that of Y is 2, 8, 5.
Both X and Y have 5 valence electrons.
X has 2 electron shells while Y has 3 electron shells.

questions.explanation: X and Y are isotopes having electronic configuration as 2, 5. Hence, both have five valence electrons.

questions.question: An electron jumps from an orbit P to Q and loses energy. The same electron when jumps from R to Q, gains energy. The increasing order of distance of these orbits from the nucleus is
questions.explanation: When an electron jumps from an orbit of lower energy to orbit of higher energy, it gains energy while when it jumps from higher energy orbit to lower energy orbit, it loses energy.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?

  1. The number of valence electrons in carbon is 6.
  2. The atoms {tex}^{40}_{18}X{/tex}, and {tex}^{40}_{20}Y{/tex} represent a pair of isotopes.
  3. Number of electrons present in the outermost shell of an atom determines the valency of the atom.
  4. C-14, N-15 and O-16 are isotones.
questions.explanation: Carbon has four valence electrons and {tex}^{40}_{18}X{/tex}, {tex}^{40}_{20}Y{/tex} are isobars.

questions.question:
Statement 1: Isobars have different chemical properties.
Statement 2: The sum of protons and neutrons in isobars is always different.

options.option: Statement 1 is true and statement 2 is false.
Both statements 1 and 2 are true but statement 2 is not the correct explanation of statement 1.
Both statements 1 and 2 are true and statement 2 is the correct explanation of statement 1.

questions.explanation: Sum of protons and neutrons in isobars is same.

questions.question: The average atomic mass of boron is 10.8 u. If the isotopic mass of B-10 is 10.013 and that of B-11 is 11.009, then the relative abundance of these isotopes is respectively
questions.explanation:
10.8 = {tex}\frac{10.013 x+(100-x) 11.009}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1080 = 10.013x + 1100.9 - 11.009x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 20.98%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Abundance of B-10 = 20.98%
Abundance of B-11 = 100 - 20.98 = 79.02%

questions.question:
Which of the following statements are incorrect regarding the elements given below?
{tex}^{39}_{19}U{/tex}, {tex}^{127}_{53}V{/tex}, {tex}^{35}_{17}W{/tex}, {tex}^{80}_{35}X{/tex}, {tex}^{40}_{18}Y{/tex}

  1. The correct order of increasing proton number is U < W < X < V < Y.
  2. The correct order of increasing mass number is W < U < Y < X < V.
  3. There is difference in the orders of proton number and mass number.
  4. The number of protons is equal to number of neutrons in all the given elements.
questions.explanation:
Atomic number = Number of protons

Mass number = Number of protons + Number of neutrons

questions.question: Hydrogen exists in three isotopic forms, {tex}{ }_1^1 \mathrm{H},{ }_1^2 \mathrm{H},{ }_1^3 \mathrm{H}{/tex} respectively known as protium, deuterium and tritium. Why are all the isotopes neutral in nature?

options.option: All the isotopes have one proton and one neutron, hence they are neutral.
All the isotopes have one electron and one proton, hence they are neutral.
All the isotopes contain same number of neutrons hence, they are electrically neutral.
Equal number of protons and neutrons in the isotopes make them neutral.

questions.explanation: Isotopes of hydrogen are neutral due to presence of one proton and one electron.

questions.question: A compound of carbon, hydrogen and nitrogen contains these elements in the ratio 9 : 1 : 3.5. If its molecular mass is 108, what is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation:
Ratio of elements C, H and N = 9 : 1 : 3.5
Whole number ratio of elements C, H and N = 18 : 2 : 7

Element Ratio Atomic mass Atomic ratio Simplest atomic ratio
C 18 12 {tex}\frac {18}{12}{/tex} = 1.5  1.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 3
H 2 1 {tex}\frac 21{/tex} = 2 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 4
N 7 14 {tex}\frac 7{14}{/tex} = 0.5 0.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 1

Thus, empirical formula of the compound = C3H4N Empirical formula mass of the compound
= 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 + 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 + 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 14 = 36 + 4 + 14 = 54
n = {tex}\frac{\text { Molecular mass }}{\text { Empirical formula mass }}{/tex} = {tex}\frac {108}{54}{/tex} = 2
Molecular formula = n {tex}\times{/tex} empirical formula
= (C3H4N)2 = C6H8N2

questions.question:
Which of the given pairs of atoms contain(s) the same number of neutrons?

  1. {tex}^{114}_{48}Cd{/tex} and {tex}^{119}_{50}Sn{/tex}
  2. {tex}^{59}_{27}Co{/tex} and {tex}^{59}_{28}Ni{/tex}
  3. {tex}^{113}_{55}Cs{/tex} and {tex}^{132}_{54}Xe{/tex}
  4. {tex}^{63}_{29}Cu{/tex} and {tex}^{65}_{29}Cu{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Number of neutrons in
Cd = 66, Sn = 69; Co = 32, Ni = 31
Cs = 78, Xe = 78; 63Cu = 34 , 65Cu = 36

questions.question:
Study the given mass spectrum of magnesium carefully.

The number of protons in 26Mg, number of neutrons in 25Mg, and the relative atomic mass of Mg are respectively
questions.explanation:
No. of protons in 26Mg = 12
No. of neutrons in 25Mg = 13
Relative atomic mass of Mg
= {tex}\frac{(24 \times 78.99)+(25 \times 10)+(26 \times 11.01)}{100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1895.76+250+286.26}{100}{/tex} = 24.32

questions.question:
Electron distribution of two elements P and Q in their outermost shell is shown below.

Atomic numbers of P and Q are respectively
questions.explanation:
Electronic configuration of P:
{tex}\begin{array}{llll} K & L & M & N \\ 2 & 8 & 8 & 6 \end{array}{/tex}
So, its atomic number = 24
Electronic configuration of Q:
{tex}\begin{array}{lll} K & L & M \\ 2 & 8 & 7 \end{array}{/tex}
So, its atomic number = 17

subtopics.name: What are Living Organisms Made Up of?

questions.question: The energy currency of cell is:
questions.explanation: The energy currency of a cell is ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate). Energy produced by a cell during cellular respiration is stored in the form of ATP molecules.

questions.question: Mitochondria are sites of
questions.explanation: Aerobic respiration

questions.question: Which organelle releases oxygen?
questions.explanation:
Chloroplasts are found in the leaves of plants. They are involved in the process of photosynthesis during which oxygen is produced and the net amount is given off.
C6H12O6 + 6CO2 {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} 6O2 + 6H2O + Energy

questions.question: Which of the following human cells lacks a nucleus?
questions.explanation: Red Blood Cells (RBC) are also called erythrocytes. Red Blood Cells in mammals anucleate i.e. they do not contain a nucleus when they are mature. This is so that the cell has maximum space for haemoglobin. In comparison, the red blood cells of other vertebrates have nuclei.

questions.question: Organisms lacking true nucleus are called:
questions.explanation: A prokaryote is a unicellular organism that lacks a membrane-bound nucleus (karyon), mitochondria, or any other membrane-bound organelle. The word prokaryote comes from Greek words - pro means "before" and karyon means a "nut or kernel".

questions.question: Plasmolysis is a plant cell is defined as:

options.option: Break down (lysis) of plasma membrane in hypotonic medium

questions.explanation: Plasmolysis is a plant cell that refers to the contraction of protoplast as a result of the loss of water from the cell. The shrinkage of cytoplasm occurs due to exo-osmosis in a hypertonic medium. A hypertonic solution is one that has a lesser concentration of water as compared to that inside the cell. During the process, there is a higher external osmotic pressure and a net flow of water from the cell through the semi-permeable cell membrane.

questions.question: Which organelle is called the 'powerhouse' of the cell?
questions.explanation: A mitochondrion is known as the "powerhouse" of the cell. It is the organelle in the cytoplasm of the cell in which the biochemical processes of respiration and energy production occur. The popular term "powerhouse of the cell" was coined by Philip Siekevitz in 1957.

questions.question: The Cell theory was proposed by:
questions.explanation: The 'Cell theory' was proposed by Matthias Jakob Schleiden and Theodor Schwann. Schleiden wrote "Contributions to our knowledge of phytogenesis" in which he stated that all parts of the plant are composed of cells. Rudolf Virchow contributed to the Cell theory by stating that 'all cells arise from pre-existing cell'.

questions.question: Which organelle is popularly known as the suicidal bag?
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are popularly known as 'suicidal bags'. They play a minor role in cell death. They were discovered and named by Belgian biologist Christian de Duve. De Duve had termed the organelles as the "suicide bags" or "suicide sacs" of the cells. He received the Nobel Prize in Medicine in 1974 for his discovery.

questions.question: The site of cellular respiration is:
questions.explanation: The mitochondrion is the site of cellular respiration inside a cell. It contains enzymes for cellular respiration. During cellular respiration, energy is released in the form of ATP (Adenosine triphosphate).

questions.question: Amoeba acquires its food through a process termed:
questions.explanation: Amoeba acquires its food through a process termed as endocytosis. Endocytosis is a form of active transport in which a cell transports molecules (such as proteins) into the cell (endo+cytosis) by engulfing them in an energy-using process. Endocytosis includes pinocytosis (cell drinking) and phagocytosis (cell eating). On the other hand, an Exocytosis is a form of active transport in which a cell transports molecules (such as proteins) out of the cell (Exo + cytosis). Plasmolysis is a plant cell that refers to the contraction of protoplast as a result of the loss of water from the cell.

questions.question: Who proposed that all cells arise from pre-existing cells?
questions.explanation: Rudolf Virchow had proposed that 'all cells arise from pre-existing cell'. Cell theory was given by Matthias Jakob Schleiden - a German botanist - along with Theodor Schwann and Rudolf Virchow.

subtopics.name: What is a Cell Made up of What is the Structural Organisation of a Cell

questions.question: We generally mount the material in the slide
questions.explanation: In the centre of slide

questions.question: 'Ribosomes' are associated with the synthesis of:
questions.explanation: 'Ribosomes' are associated with the synthesis of proteins from amino acids. Proteins are required by a cell for functions such as repair or directing chemical processes.

questions.question: The membrane surrounding a vacuole in a plant cell is known as:
questions.explanation: Tonoplast is the membrane that bounds the chief vacuole of a plant cell. It is also known as the 'vacuolar membrane'. It separates the vacuolar contents from the cytoplasm of the cell.

questions.question: Cell-wall of plant cell is made up of cellulose. Which of the following is not able to digest cellulose?
questions.explanation: Cellulose is a complex material which cannot be digested by human beings. Only ruminants can digest it. for ex: cow

questions.question: Cell arises from the pre-existing cell was stated by
questions.explanation: Rudolf Virchow presented the idea in 1855 that every cell arises from another (pre-existing) cell. This was an addition to the cell theory that was proposed earlier by Matthias Jakob Schleiden and Theodor Schwann.

questions.question: Animal cell lacking nuclei would also lack in:
questions.explanation: An animal cell that does not contain nuclei will also lack chromosomes. Chromosomes are present inside the nucleus of a cell. Chromosomes contain information for the inheritance of features from parents to the next generation in the form of DNA (Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid) molecules.

questions.question: The number of chromosomes present in the frog is:
questions.explanation: There are 26 chromosomes (13 pairs) in the cells of a frog. These chromosomes are present inside the nucleus of a cell. Chromosomes contain information for the inheritance of features from parents to the next generation in the form of DNA (Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid) molecules.

questions.question: Ribosomes are the centre for:
questions.explanation: Protein synthesis involves the formation of amino acid chains according to information present on the DNA. The ribosomes, which are present in all active cells, are the sites for the manufacture of proteins. The proteins manufactured by the ribosomes are sent to other organelles in the cell. Some proteins function as enzymes and hormones.

questions.question: A cell has 10 chromosomes. After mitotic cell division, the number of chromosomes in the daughter cell will be:
questions.explanation: Mitosis is a part of the cell cycle where replicated chromosomes are separated into two new nuclei. During mitotic division, the number of chromosomes in the daughter cells remains the same. Therefore, a cell having 10 chromosomes will produce daughter cells that have 10 chromosomes each.

questions.question: Where are proteins synthesised inside the cells?
questions.explanation: The Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum (RER) has ribosomes attached to its surface. Ribosomes are the sites of protein manufacture. They are associated with the synthesis of proteins from amino acids.

questions.question: The energy currency of the cell is:
questions.explanation: Adenosine triphosphate (ATP) is called as the energy currency of the cell. The energy produced by a cell during internal respiration is stored in the form of ATP molecules. ATP breaks into ADP and phosphate to produce energy during cellular processes.

questions.question: "Viruses are non-cellular organisms" - this statement is a:
questions.explanation: It is true that viruses are non-cellular organisms. Viruses do not contain cell membranes and other cellular organelles. Viruses are the link between the living and the non-living. They do not show any characteristics of life until they enter the body of a host. They then use the host cell machinery for reproduction.

questions.question: The technical term for the “Group of similar cells performing a specific function” is:
questions.explanation: A group of similar cells performing a specific function and having a common origin is called tissue. Tissues form organs to perform a particular function. Organs form organ systems.

questions.question: The number of lenses in a compound microscope is:
questions.explanation: The compound microscope contains two lenses that magnify the object. It uses a lens (called the objective lens) close to the object being viewed to collect light which focuses on a real image of the object inside the microscope. The image is then magnified by a second lens (called the eyepiece) that gives the observer an enlarged inverted virtual image of the object.

questions.question: Amoeba acquires its food through:
questions.explanation: Amoeba acquires its food by the process of endocytosis with the help of finger-like projections called pseudopodia (Pseudo means false; podia means feet). The flexibility of the plasma membrane enables amoeba to use pseudopodia to engulf food and other material from its environment.

questions.question: Living cells were discovered by
questions.explanation: A.V. Leeuwenhoek in 1674 with the improved microscope, discovered free-living cells of algae Spirogyra in pond water for the first time.

questions.question: The well defined nucleus is absent in ________.
questions.explanation: Prokaryotic cells do not have a nuclear membrane, and cell organelles are also not well enveloped.

questions.question:
Which of the following is an incorrect pair?

  1. Lysosome - Secretory granules.
  2. Nucleus - Brain of the cell
  3. Mitochondria - a powerhouse of the cell
  4. Chloroplast - Kitchen of the cell
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are the suicidal bags that kill the cells which are turned off or damaged and became non-functional. They do not secrete anything rather possess some lytic enzymes membrane-bound.

questions.question: Chlorophyll is present in ________.
questions.explanation: A thylakoid is a sheet-like membrane-bound structure that is the site of the light-dependent photosynthesis reactions in chloroplasts and cyanobacteria. It is the site that contains the chlorophyll used to absorb light and use it for biochemical reactions.

questions.question: Ribosomes are made up of ________.
questions.explanation: Ribosomes consist of two major components: the small ribosomal subunit, which reads the RNA, and the large subunit, which joins amino acids to form a polypeptide chain. Each subunit is composed of one or more ribosomal RNA (rRNA) molecules and a variety of ribosomal proteins (r-protein).

questions.question: Which of the following is absent in plant cells?
questions.explanation: A centriole is an organelle that helps cells divide, or make copies of themselves. Centrioles are only found in animal cells. All centrioles are made of protein strands called microtubules.

questions.question: Colourless plastids are known as ________.
questions.explanation: There are three major types of plastids- chloroplast, chromoplast, and leucoplast. The leucoplast is non-pigmented plastids (Leuco = white; plast = living). Their main function is to store food materials. They are not involved in synthesis activities that's why to lack any special pigmentation.

questions.question: Which of the following is known as the "physical basis of life"?
questions.explanation: Protoplasm According to Huxley is the physical basis of life. Inside the cell wall of living cells, the living substance is known as protoplasm and it is a thick fluid or jellylike substance in texture.

questions.question: Which of the following acts as a garbage disposal system of the cell?
questions.explanation: The lysosome is a membrane-bound organelle found in nearly all animal cells. They are spherical vesicles that contain hydrolytic enzymes that can break down many kinds of biomolecules. It is also known as suicidal bags or the Garbage disposal system as it cleans the degenerating cells inside the body.

questions.question: The most abundant material on the plant cell wall is:
questions.explanation: Cellulose is an important structural component of the primary cell wall of green plants, many forms of algae, and the oomycetes. Some species of bacteria secrete it to form biofilms. Thus, cellulose is the most abundant organic polymer on Earth.

questions.question: The membrane of the Golgi apparatus has connections with those of:
questions.explanation: The Golgi apparatus functions as a factory in which proteins received from the Endoplasmic reticulum are further processed and sorted for transport to their eventual destinations: lysosomes, the plasma membrane, or secretion.

questions.question: The major function of the Golgi apparatus is:
questions.explanation: The Golgi apparatus is the organelle in which proteins are prepared for secretion and undergo terminal glycosylation.

questions.question: The site of detoxification in liver cells is:
questions.explanation: SER i.e. the smooth endoplasmic reticulum lacks ribosomes and functions in lipid manufacture and metabolism, the production of steroid hormones, and detoxification.

questions.question: The cell organelles with digestive enzymes are:
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are the organelle in the cytoplasm of eukaryotic cells containing hydrolytic enzymes enclosed in a membrane.

questions.question: 70-80% of the volume of a mature plant cell is occupied by:
questions.explanation: Vacuoles occupy a very large part of the cell volume in plants. Upton 95% of cellular volume can be occupied by them.

questions.question: Most of the substances in the living world are transported across the cell membrane by the process of:
questions.explanation: Osmosis is the spontaneous net movement of solvent molecules through a semi-permeable membrane (example- cell membrane) into a region of higher solute concentration, in the direction that tends to equalize the solute concentrations on the two sides.

questions.question: The structure which forms a barrier between the protoplasm of the cell and its external environment in an animal cell is:
questions.explanation: The cell membrane (also known as the plasma membrane) is a biological membrane that separates the interior of all cells from the outside environment (the extracellular space). It consists of a lipid bilayer with embedded proteins.

questions.question: Which among the following is concerned with the synthesis and transport of lipids within the cell?
questions.explanation: Smooth endoplasmic reticulum synthesises lipids while rough endoplasmic reticulum synthesise proteins.

questions.question: Lysosomes are the reservoirs of:
questions.explanation: Lysosomes enclose digestive enzymes to digest the degenerative cells and tissues

questions.question: The cell organelles with membrane-bound cisternae located near the nucleus of the cell are
questions.explanation: Golgi apparatus is a complex of vesicles and folded membranes within the cytoplasm of most eukaryotic cells, involved in secretion and intracellular transport.

questions.question: The compounds synthesised near the ER are packaged and dispatched to various sites inside and outside the cell through:
questions.explanation: Golgi apparatus is an organelle present in most eukaryotic cells. It is made up of membrane-bound sacs and transport molecules from the endoplasmic reticulum to different locations.

questions.question: The structure of the nuclear membrane is suitable for:

options.option: the organisation of spindle for nuclear division

questions.explanation: The nuclear membrane allows the nucleocytoplasmic exchange of materials as the membrane behaves as a semipermeable membrane.

questions.question: Viruses do not show any characteristic of living until they enter a living cell, because of the absence of:
questions.explanation: Viruses are only crystalline genetic materials in the form of RNA and DNA. They lack any membrane. After getting incorporated in the host DNA they become functional otherwise remain inactive.

questions.question: The basic building units of an onion bulb, are called ________.
questions.explanation: Cells are the basic and fundamental unit of life, in both plants and animals.

questions.question: A common feature of chloroplasts and mitochondria is that both are
questions.explanation: Chloroplast and mitochondria have their own DNA and ribosomes as they are capable of transcription and secreting proteins.They possess their own genetic material along with the nucleus.

questions.question: The statement 'cells arise only from pre-existing cells' was given by:
questions.explanation: This statement was proposed by Rudolf Virchow in 1855. This theory is called Virchow's theory or Cell lineage theory. This the theory made as a result of objection to the Cell theory.

questions.question: Rough endoplasmic reticulum helps in the synthesis of:
questions.explanation: The ribosomes on the RER are synthesizing protein by definition, meaning that ribosomes do not exist in the cell unless they are synthesizing a protein from an mRNA template.

questions.question: Lysosomes are formed by:
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are manufactured and budded into the cytoplasm by the Golgi apparatus with hydrolytic enzymes inside. The enzymes that are within the lysosome are made in the rough endoplasmic reticulum, which are then delivered to the Golgi apparatus to synthesise lysosomes.

questions.question: A eukaryotic nucleus has a:
questions.explanation: The bounding structure of the eukaryotic nucleus. Composed of two phospholipid bilayers with the outer one connected to the endoplasmic reticulum. Double membrane structure riddled with pores that surround deoxyribonucleic acid in eukaryotes. The nuclear pores, like guards at an important government building, are very strict.

questions.question: The functional units of the Golgi apparatus are:
questions.explanation: Golgi apparatus is a membrane-bound organelle of eukaryotic cells (cells with clearly defined nuclei) that is made up of a series of flattened, stacked pouches called cisternae. The Golgi apparatus is responsible for transporting, modifying, and packaging proteins and lipids into vesicles for delivery to targeted destinations.

questions.question: The cell sap in plant cells consists of:
questions.explanation: The liquid found inside the plant cell vacuole is referred to as the cell sap and it is a dilute fluid consisting of water, amino acids, glucose i.e. (water + inorganic substances), water may also be inhabited with a negligible amount of salts because it somehow depends on the mineral excess inside the body of plants. The excess material is temporarily stored inside the cell sap. Thus, vacuoles act as storehouses in plants.

questions.question: Which of the following cell functions will stop, if its ribosomes are destroyed?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are the site of protein synthesis on rough endoplasmic reticulums. Long-chain of polypeptides are synthesized on the ribosomes.

questions.question: Which of the following can be made into crystal?
questions.explanation: Viruses are considered as an intermediate between living and non-living cells because they cannot metabolite and reproduce on their own. They can reproduce only when enters in a host's body. They are an exception to cell theory. A virus crystal is a collection of thousands of viruses. A viral crystal is a pore collection used for chemical studies.

questions.question: A cell will swell up if

options.option: The concentration of water molecules in the cell is higher than the concentration of water molecules is the surrounding medium.
The concentration of water molecules does not matter.
The concentration of water molecules is the same in the cell and in the surrounding medium.
The concentration of water molecules in the surrounding medium is higher than water molecules concentration in the cell.

questions.explanation: A cell will swell up if it is placed in the hypotonic solution. This is so because the concentration of water molecules in the surrounding medium is higher than water molecules concentration in the cell So, the water molecules move from external solution into cell sap.

questions.question: Chromosomes are made up of
questions.explanation: Each chromosome is made up of DNA tightly coiled many times around proteins called histones that support its structure.

questions.question: Plasmolysis in a plant cell is defined as

options.option: break down (lysis) of plasma membrane in hypotonic medium
shrinkage of cytoplasm in hypertonic medium

questions.explanation: Plasmolysis is mainly known as shrinking of cell membrane in hypertonic solution and great pressure.

questions.question: Which of the following are covered by a single membrane?
questions.explanation: Vacuole is a cavity within the cytoplasm of a cell, surrounded by a single membrane and containing fluid, food, or metabolic waste. Mitochondria, Nucleus and Plastids are surrounded by double membrane.

questions.question: Which cell organelle plays a crucial role in detoxifying many poison and drugs in a cell?
questions.explanation: Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulumis not only plays a role in detoxification but also regulates and releases calcium ions. These are the network of tubular membranes within the cytoplasm of the cell. They are involved in the transport of materials.

questions.question: The proteins and lipids, essential for building the cell membrane, are manufactured by
questions.explanation: The proteins and lipids, essential for building the cell membrane, are synthesized by the rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER). The ribosomes attached to the RER help in this process.

questions.question: The undefined nuclear region of prokaryotes are also known as
questions.explanation: The undefined nuclear region of prokaryotes are also known as nucleoid which means similar to nucleus. It is present as there is no nuclear membrane covering in the nuclear region.

questions.question: The cell organelle involved in forming complex sugars from simple sugars are
questions.explanation:
Golgi bodies consist of a system of membrane-bound vesicles arranged in stacks parallel to each other called cisterns. These membranes have connections with the membrane of endoplasmic reticulum (ER). Functions:

  1. It also stores, modifies and helps in the packaging of products in vesicles.
  2. In some cases, complex sugars may be made from simple sugars in it.
  3. It also helps in the formation of lysosomes.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a function of vacuole?

options.option: Providing turgidity and rigidity to the cell

questions.explanation:
Vacuoles appear to have three main functions, they:

  1. contribute to the rigidity of the plant using water to develop hydrostatic pressure
  2. store nutrient and non-nutrient chemicals
  3. break down complex molecules.

Locomotion is not a function of vacuole.

questions.question: Amoeba acquires its food through a process, termed
questions.explanation: Amoeba acquires its food through the process of Endocytosis. Actually it has cytoplasmic projections called psedupodia (false feet) that extend out of its body. It moves the pseudopodia towards the food and take it in its body through the process of endocytosis.

questions.question: Cell wall of which one of these is not made up of cellulose?
questions.explanation: Bacterial cell wall is the most important site for attack by antibiotics. It also is responsible for immunological distinction and immunological variation among strains of bacteria.

questions.question: Silver nitrate solution is used to study
questions.explanation: Silver nitrate is an inorganic, irritant, colourless, water-soluble, poisonous, clear, crystalline compound. It is used in photography and silver plating. Silver nitrate solution is used to study Golgi apparatus. Golgi apparatus is an organelle in eukaryotic cells.

questions.question: Organelle other than nucleus, containing DNA is
questions.explanation: A cell can contain DNA in an area other than the nucleus and the mitochondria.

questions.question: Kitchen of the cells
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts are special structures present only in plant cells. With the help of this chlorophyll, the plants prepare their food in the presence of water, sunlight and oxygen. Hence, chloroplasts are called the kitchen of a plant cell.

questions.question: Lipid molecules in the cell are synthesized by
questions.explanation: The smooth endoplasmic reticulum lacks ribosomes and functions in lipid manufacture and metabolism, the production of steroid hormones, and detoxification.

questions.question: Cell theory was given by
questions.explanation: Cell theory was eventually formulated in 1839. This is usually credited to Matthias Schleiden and Theodor Schwann.

questions.question: The structure/organelle of a cell that functions as a passage for intracellular transport as well as a manufacturing surface is:
questions.explanation: The SER helps in the manufacture of fat molecules, or lipids, important for cell function. One function of the ER is to serve as channels for the transport of materials (especially proteins) between various regions of the cytoplasm or between the cytoplasm and the nucleus.

questions.question: If a plant cell is kept in a hypotonic solution, it will:
questions.explanation: The cell is said to be turgid when the plant cell wall becomes rigid and stretched by an increase in the volume of vacuoles due to the absorption of water when placed in a hypotonic solution.

questions.question: Analyze the statements and pick up the right one regarding mitochondrial membranes from the following:

options.option: Both the inner and the outer membranes are almost equal in length
The inner membrane is longer than the outer membrane
The outer membrane is longer than the inner membrane

questions.explanation: The inner membrane, which has a larger surface area than the outer membrane, contains features referred to as cristae (literally, crests) which have long been represented as simple infoldings of this membrane.

questions.question: The cell organelles (other than the nucleus) which contain DNA are:
questions.explanation: In eukaryotes, DNA is generally membrane-bound. Hence in eukaryotes, it is found within the nucleus, mitochondria (animal cell), and plastids (chloroplasts, plant cells).

questions.question: The primary function of smooth endoplasmic reticulum in liver cells is:
questions.explanation: Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (SER) is responsible for the synthesis and repair of membranes. It also has a detoxification function.

questions.question: Engulfing of food materials or foreign bodies by cells like Amoeba is called:
questions.explanation: The cell membranes flexibility allows the cell engulf in food and other material from its external environment. This process is known as endocytosis.

questions.question: In the plant cells, many substances important for life are stored in:
questions.explanation: Vacuoles are storage sacs for solid otherwise liquid content. In the plant cells, it provides rigidity and turgidity to the cell. In single-celled organisms, vacuoles store food, e.g., Amoeba.

questions.question: A prokaryotic cell does not possess:
questions.explanation: Prokaryotes do have their genomic DNA concentrated and localized to a small area within the cell (nucleoid region). So it's not entirely accurate to say that prokaryotes don't have a nucleus.

questions.question: The only cell organelle seen in prokaryotic cell is
questions.explanation: Unlike eukaryotic cell, a prokaryotic cell lacks membrane-bound organelles like plastids, mitochondria and endoplasmic reticulum but smaller and randomly scattered ribosomes are seen.

questions.question: Organelle without a cell membrane is
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are non membrane bound organelles that are found freely occurring in the cytoplasm.

questions.question: Lysosomes arises from
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are membrane bound vesicles that arise from the  Golgi apparatus.

questions.question:
Which of these options are not a function of Ribosomes?

  1. It helps in manufacture of protein molecules
  2. It helps in manufacture of enzymes
  3. It helps in manufacture of hormones
  4. It helps in manufacture of starch molecules
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are involved in protein synthesis. Enzymes are also proteins. Hence, ribosomes also make enzymes.

questions.question: Which of these is not related to endoplasmic reticulum?

options.option: It transports materials between various regions in cytoplasm.
It behaves as transport channel for proteins between nucleus and cytoplasm.
It can be the site of energy generation.
It can be the site for some biochemical activities of the cell

questions.explanation: Mitochondria is the site of energy generation. It is not generated in the endoplasmic reticulum.

questions.question: Following are a few definitions of osmosis read carefully and select the correct definition.

options.option: Movement of water molecules from a region of higher concentration to a region of lower concentration through a semipermeable membrane
Movement of solute molecules from lower concentration to higher concentration of solution through a semipermeable membrane
Movement of solvent molecules from higher concentration to lower concentration of solution through a permeable membrane
Movement of solvent molecules from its higher concentration to lower concentration

questions.explanation: Osmosis is the passive movement of water or any other solvent molecules from a region of higher water concentration to a region of lower water concentration through a semipermeable membrane.

questions.question: 1 {tex}\mu{/tex}m is
questions.explanation: The value of 1 {tex}\mu{/tex}m is 10-6 m.

questions.question: Robert Brown is a well known scientist as he discovered ________.
questions.explanation: Nucleus

questions.question: Refer to the given figure and select the incorrect statement regarding X, Y and Z.

options.option: Y is arranged in characteristic pattern in different cells and bear oxysomes.
Z contains respiratory enzymes, lipids, RNA, etc.
X is freely permeable to small molecules and ions.
Y contains respiratory enzymes, lipids, RNA, etc.

questions.explanation: In the given figure of mitochondrion, X, Y and Z are inner membrane, crista and matrix respectively. The inner membrane is selectively permeable to small molecules and ions.

questions.question: Plant cell does not burst in a hypotonic solution because ________.

options.option: Its cell sap is rich in cellulose

questions.explanation: Cell wall exerts a pressure on the cytoplasm in the opposite direction. It prevents bursting of the cell in a hypotonic solution and limits endosmosis to a certain extent.

questions.question: The kitchen of the cell is
questions.explanation: The organelle in option (A) is chloroplast. It traps solar energy which is used for manufacturing of food. Therefore, it is called the kitchen of the cell.

questions.question: X is present in all eukaryotic cells except mammalian RBC, sieve tube cells of phloem and tracheids and vessels of xylem. X is ________.
questions.explanation: Nucleus

questions.question: Unicellular algae X and Y of the same species were taken and chloroplasts were removed from cell X. After some time, they both were kept in bright sunlight for few hours and then iodine test was performed on them. What will be the results?

options.option: Both X and Y will turn blue-black.
Both X and Y will remain as they originally were.

questions.explanation: Algae contains chloroplast. Under the sunlight, algae will photosynthesise and produces starch. As cell X lacked chloroplast, it will not make starch while the cell Y will make it. Starch turns blue-black in the presence of iodine. Cell Y made starch by photosynthesis, thus it will turn blue-black with iodine solution.

questions.question: All members of the Gupta family can roll their tongue. Which of the parts labelled in the given figure carries information regarding this characteristic?

Study the given figure carefully. In which direction the net movement of water will take place?

[Key : O Water molecule; ® Solute molecule]

options.option: Both From solution 1 to solution 2 and From solution 2 to solution 1

questions.explanation: The movement of water from a region of its higher concentration to a region of its lower concentration through a semi-permeable membrane is called osmosis. Since, concentration of water is high in solution 1, therefore, water will move from solution 1 to solution 2 by the process of osmosis.

questions.question:
Statement 1: Mitochondria are known as the power house of the cell.
Statement 2: Mitochondria are used to bring about stepwise oxidation of food stuffs and release energy.

options.option: Both statements 1 and 2 are true and statement 2 is the correct explanation of statement 1.
Statement 1 is true but statement 2 is false.
Both statements 1 and 2 are true but statement 2 is not the correct explanation of statement 1.

questions.explanation: Both statements 1 and 2 are true and statement 2 is the correct explanation of statement 1.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is incorrect regarding endoplasmic reticulum?

options.option: RER helps in the synthesis of proteins.
ER helps in the transport of materials from one part of the cell to another.
SER helps in detoxification in the liver of vertebrates.

questions.explanation: None of these

questions.question: Which of the following is correct for the given figure?

options.option: The part labelled ‘a’ is the site of dark reaction.
The parts labelled ‘a’, ‘b' and ‘c’, all possess photosynthetic pigments such as chlorophyll.
The part labelled ‘b’ is called intergranal thylakoid.

questions.explanation: The given figure is of chloroplast. The part labelled ‘a’ is stroma. The part labelled ‘b’ is called grana where light reaction of photosynthesis takes place. The part labelled ‘c’ is intergranal thylakoid. Photosynthetic pigments such as chlorophyll are present only in thylakoid membranes (grana + intergranal thylakoids) and not in stroma or nongranal thylakoids.

questions.question:
Select the incorrect statement.

  1. Osmosis is a slow process, occurs down the concentration gradient and does not expend energy.
  2. Electron microscope uses very high voltage electricity. It uses electromagnets instead of glass lenses and beam of electrons instead of light.
  3. A semipermeable membrane does not allow both solvent and solute molecules to pass through it.
  4. Active transport of materials is rapid and usually occurs against the concentration gradient involving carrier proteins and energy in the form of ATP.
questions.explanation: A semipermeable membrane allows only solvent molecules to pass through it.

questions.question: Among the following cell organelles P, Q, R and S, which is the odd one out?

options.option: S, because the others are membrane bound cell organelles.
P, because the others are involved in cell secretion.
Q, because the others are involved in energy production.
R, because the others are involved in protein storage.

questions.explanation: Figures 'P', ‘Q’, ‘R and ‘S’ represent mitochondrion, vacuole, chloroplast and ribosome, respectively

questions.question:
Four strips are cut from a fresh potato. The length of each strip is measured. One strip is placed in water and others in different concentrations of sugar solution. After an hour, the strips were measured again. The results are shown in the table. Which of the liquids P, Q, R and S is water?

Liquid Original length of strip (mm) Final length of strip (mm)
P 75 75
Q 78 80
R 82 80
S 86 85
questions.explanation: Sugar solution is hypertonic to cell sap of potato, whereas water acts as a hypotonic solution to it. When the potato strip is placed in water, its cells will gain water by osmosis, resulting in increase in size of potato strip. When the potato strips are put in sugar solution of different concentrations, the cells will lose water resulting in decrease in the size of strip. As only potato strip placed in liquid ‘Q’ shows increase in length, this shows that liquid ‘Q’ is water.

questions.question:
Take a clean glass slide and put few drops of water on it. Now place a complete Rheo leaf on water droplets and examine the cells of leaf under the high power of compound microscope. Put a few drops of concentrated salt/sugar solution on the mounted Rheo leaf on the glass slide. Wait for few minutes and again observe the leaf under the high power of microscope.
What will be your observation after few minutes?

options.option: Cell contents are separated from the cell wall.
Cytoplasm along with plasma membrane has come to lie on one side of cell wall.
A clear space is seen between the cell wall and protoplast of the cell.

questions.explanation: Shrinkage of the protoplast of a plant cell from its cell wall under the influence of a hypertonic solution is called plasmolysis. If we put a Rheo leaf in a concentrated salt/ sugar solution, it will cause exosmosis or withdrawal of water from cytoplasm and then the central vacuole of cell. As a result, the size of cytoplasm as well as central vacuole decreases and hence protoplast reduces. The plasma membrane gets withdrawn from the cell wall and a clear space is seen between the cell wall and protoplast of the cell.

questions.question: A unicellular protist X, which has contractile vacuole to remove excess intracellular water, was placed in salt solution of increasing osmolarity. The given graph shows the rate of contraction of vacuole to pump out excess water against osmolarity of solution. Select the option that gives the correct explanation of the data.

options.option: The contractile vacuole is less efficient in solution of high osmolarity because of reduced respiration and less production of ATP.
The rate of contraction increases as osmolarity decreases because amount of water entering X by osmosis increases.
In an isotonic solution there is no diffusion of water in or out of the X, so the contraction rate is zero.
At higher osmolarity, more salt diffuses into X, therefore lower rates of contractions are required.

questions.explanation: The rate of contraction increases as osmolarity decreases because amount of water entering X by osmosis increases.

questions.question: Vertical growth in plants takes place by :-
questions.explanation: Apical meristem is responsible for the growth of root and shoot of a plant. The division of these cells always results in primary (vertical) growth.

questions.question: Which of these components of blood fight infection?
questions.explanation: WBC, also called leucocyte are involved in the protection of the body against infectious diseases and foreign invaders.

questions.question: In desert plants, the rate of water loss gets reduced due to the presence of:-
questions.explanation: The cuticle is a thick waxy layer that prevents the loss of water from the surface of the leaf.

questions.question: Cartilage does not found in
questions.explanation: Cartilage is soft bone work as connective tissue found in ear, nose, and larynx.lt provides support and flexibility. The kidney is a muscular structure it is an excretory organ consist of nephrons.

questions.question: Which of these types of cells is most likely to divide?
questions.explanation:
The meristematic tissue is of the following types:

  1. Apical meristem (responsible for vertical growth of root and shoot)
  2. Lateral meristem (responsible for the increase in girth of a stem)
  3. Intercalary meristem (responsible for the branching in a plant).

The three types of growth take place due to division in tissues.

questions.question: Companion cells are associated with:-
questions.explanation: Companion cell is a specialized parenchyma cell, located in the phloem of flowering plant in close association with sieve tube.

questions.question: Which tissue has chloroplast in cells?
questions.explanation: Chlorenchyma is a parenchyma tissue, consist of chloroplasts and performs photosynthesis found in the mesophyll of leaves.

questions.question: The meristematic tissue is found:
questions.explanation: Meristematic tissue divides rapidly which results in the growth of root and shoot apex.

questions.question: Movement of the passage of food in the intestine is caused by the contraction of:
questions.explanation: Unstriated muscles also called involuntary or smooth muscles, do nor work under our control. It secretes enzymes and digestive juices hence perform digestion regularly.

questions.question: A long tubular outgrowth of a nerve cell which conducts impulses away from the cell body is termed as :-
questions.explanation: Axon

questions.question: What is the function of White Blood Corpuscles:
questions.explanation: WBC protects the body against diseases because they produce antibodies that help in fighting against invaders.

questions.question: A person met with an accident in which 2 long bones of hand were dislocated. Which among the following may be the possible reason?
questions.explanation: Ligament is a connective tissue that connects one bone to another .

questions.question: What constitutes thickening in collenchyma?
questions.explanation: Collenchyma cells are elongated cells with irregularly thick cell walls that provide support and structure. Their thick cell walls are composed of the compounds cellulose and pectin. These cells are often found under the epidermis or the outer layer of cells in young stems and in leaf veins.

questions.question: Cork is impervious to water because it has deposition of
questions.explanation: The walls of cork cells are heavily thickened with organic substance suberin. Suberin is a fatty, waxy substance that covers the thin walls of the cell and prevents the flow of water, gases, or any nutrients.

questions.question: Which of the following conducts water and minerals in plants?
questions.explanation: Xylem is composed of tracheids, vessels, xylem parenchyma, and xylem fibres. The cells of xylem are dead; except the cells of xylem parenchyma. Tracheids and vessels are tubular structures and thus they provide a channel for conduction of water and minerals.

questions.question: Squamous epithelium is found in _____
questions.explanation: Simple squamous epithelium lines the air sacs, or alveoli of the lungs. The alveoli are sites where the air is exchanged in the lungs. Simple squamous epithelial cells in the alveoli allow oxygen from the air to enter the blood in the capillaries of the lung. Carbon dioxide, a waste product, passes across the epithelium of the alveoli to be removed from the body.

questions.question: Large intercellular spaces are present in _____.
questions.explanation: There are large intercellular spaces between the cells. Intercellular spaces are filled with a large amount of extracellular materials formed of insoluble protein fibres lying in an amorphous, transparent ground substance called matrix. The ageing of an animal body is associated with deterioration in its connective tissues. Connective tissue supports and binds other tissues of the body.

questions.question: Ligament connects _____.
questions.explanation: A ligament is a fibrous connective tissue which attaches bone to bone, and usually serves to hold structures together and keep them stable.

questions.question: Fibrinogen is absent in _____.
questions.explanation: Plasma is that part of the blood, which contains blood clotting agent called as fibrinogen, while serum is the fluid part of the blood and does not contain clotting agent (fibrinogen). The plasma and serum can be extracted from the centrifugation of blood. The serum is obtained after the clotting of blood, while plasma can be obtained before the coagulation of the blood.

questions.question: Intercalated disc is present in ______.
questions.explanation: The heart of human beings is made up of cardiac muscles which are different from smooth and skeletal muscles. Inside the cardiac muscles are the intercalated discs which are known to join various adjacent cells together. There are a number of functions of intercalated discs. It is only when it functions properly that the heart can work effectively.

questions.question: Heart muscles are _____.
questions.explanation: Cardiac Muscles are commonly called as Heart muscles. These are cylindrical, branched, striated, and uni-nucleate and Involuntary in nature.

questions.question: Haversian canals are present in _____.
questions.explanation: Haversian canals are found in the bone matrix of a long bone, like humerus of mammals. Haversian canals contain artery and veins, osteoblasts in areolar tissue, nerves, and lymph. It is also called an osteon.

questions.question: Which of the following is non – vascular?
questions.explanation: Epithelial tissue is composed of cells laid together in sheets with the cells tightly connected to one another. Epithelial layers are non – vascular but innervated.

questions.question: Which of the following is a dead cell?
questions.explanation: Tracheids are dead and lignified cells. The deposition of lignin on the cell wall is responsible to form a different type of thickenings.

questions.question: Chloroplasts may occur in
questions.explanation: The main function of parenchyma is to provide support and to store food. In some plant parts, parenchyma has chlorophyll as well. In that case, parenchyma carries out photosynthesis and is then termed as chlorenchyma. Collenchyma tissue contains chloroplast and carry out photosynthesis.

questions.question: The plant tissue provides mechanical strength and consists of living cells, is
questions.explanation: Collenchyma cells are refractile, non lignified, living cells with pectocellulose thickening in specific areas of walls, and without intercellular spaces. It provides mechanical strength with flexibility thus allows bending. Besides, it shows growth and elongation of organs, photosynthesize, and stores food and prevents tearing of leaves.

questions.question: Which of the following tissues of plants provides flexibility and strength for bending and swaying?
questions.explanation: Collenchyma is a strong and flexible simple tissue to provide mechanical strength to the growing organs.

questions.question: Lignified or thickened cell wall is a characteristic feature of
questions.explanation: The xylem is one of the conductive tissues in plants. It is a complex tissue composed of many types of cells. The main function of xylem is to conduct water and minerals from roots to leaves. The secondary xylem also provides mechanical support due to the presence of a thick lignified cell wall.

questions.question: The conducting cells of xylem are
questions.explanation: There are two types of conducting cells in xylem, tracheids and vessel elements. Both have thick lignified secondary walls and are dead at maturity. These cells create hollow cylinders that have high tensile strength. Materials moving within the xylem are under tension.

questions.question: Bones are connected to muscles at the joints by
questions.explanation: The bone is a connective tissue with a hard matrix, composed of calcium and phosphorus. A bone is connected by muscle with connective tissue, called a tendon.

questions.question: _____ smoothens the bone surface at the joints.
questions.explanation: Cartilage smoothens the bone surface at the joints. The cartilage is a connective tissue with a solid matrix composed of proteins and sugars. It is commonly seen in the nose, ear, trachea, and larynx.

questions.question: Which of the following tissues has dead cells?
questions.explanation: Sclerenchyma Tissue makes the plant hard and stiff, thickened due to lignin and no inter cellular space. Cells of this tissue are dead and commonly seen in the husk of coconut.

questions.question: A person met with an accident in which two long bones of hand were dislocated. Which among the following may be the possible reason?
questions.explanation: Dislocation of joint occurs when there is an abnormal separation in joint, which are held together by ligament. Therefore ligament break may result in dislocation of bone. Areolar tissues join skin to muscles, fills spaces inside organs and is found around muscles, blood vessels and nerves. Hence are not concerned with bones.

questions.question: Meristematic tissues in plants are
questions.explanation: Meristematic tissues consists of actively dividing cells and is present in the growing regions of plants, e.g., the tips of roots and stems. The cells of meristematic tissue are round, oval, polygonal or rectangular. They are packed closely without intercellular spaces, have thin cellulose walls, dense cytoplasm and prominent nuclei. Vacuoles are almost absent in such cells because they are completely filled with sap.

questions.question: Which is not a function of epidermis?
questions.explanation: Skin has three layers: The epidermis, the outermost layer of skin, provides a waterproof barrier and creates our skin tone. The dermis, beneath the epidermis, contains tough connective tissue, hair follicles, and sweat glands. The deeper subcutaneous tissue (hypodermis) is made of fat and connective tissue

questions.question: Cartilage is not found in
questions.explanation: The cartilage is a connective tissue with solid matrix composed of proteins and sugars. It is commonly seen in nose, ear, trachea, and larynx. Cartilage is not found in kidney.

questions.question: Fats are stored in human body as
questions.explanation: Adipose connective tissue is filled with fat globules for the storage of fat. It acts as insulator.

questions.question: Bone matrix is rich in
questions.explanation: The bone is a connective tissue with hard matrix, composed of calcium and phosphorus. A bone is connected by another bone with another connective tissue called ligaments. A bone is connected by muscle with another connective tissue called tendon.

questions.question: Contractile proteins are found in
questions.explanation: Contractile proteins are found in muscles, as they are associated with the movement of body or limbs. The contraction and relaxation of contractile proteins, present in muscles bring about movements of limbs, internal organs, etc.

questions.question: Voluntary muscles are found in
questions.explanation: Voluntary muscles are the muscles, which are under our complete control for, e.g., the working and movement of limbs. On the other hand involuntary muscles are controlled by hypothalamus, i.e., they are regulated rhythmically, e.g., alimentary canal, iris of the eye and bronchi of lungs.

questions.question: Nervous tissue is not found in
questions.explanation: Tendons are the connective tissue, which join skeletal muscles to bones, hence nervous tissue is absent is them. Nervous tissue is specialised to transmit messages within our body. It contains highly specialised unit cells called nerve cells or neurons. They have the ability to receive stimuli from within or outside the body and to conduct (send) impulses (signals) to different parts of the body. Brain, spinal cord are all associated with the regulation of body activity and are composed of nervous tissue.

questions.question: Nerve cell does not contain
questions.explanation:
Each nerve cell or neuron Is composed of three parts

  1. Cyton or cell body It contains central nucleus and cytoplasm with characteristic deeply stained particles called Nissl’s granules (i.e., clumps of ribosome).
  2. Dendron These are short processes arising from cyton and further branching into dendrites.
  3. Axon It is a single long cylindrical process of uniform diameter which forms fine branches terminally. The dendrites receives impulses and the axon takes impulses away from the cell body.

questions.question: The columnar epithelial cells possess cilia in
questions.explanation: Columnar epithelium in the intestine and columnar epithelium with cilia in the lining of the respiratory tract.

questions.question: Branched involuntary muscles fibres are found in
questions.explanation: Involuntary muscles are found in walls of hollow tubular organs like an alimentary canal, ducts of glands, urogenital ducts, and blood vessels except the heart. They show slow contractions but remain contracted for a long period of time.

questions.question: Chlorenchyma and aerenchyma are modified/specialised
questions.explanation: Parenchyma cells containing chloroplasts are collectively termed as chlorenchyma. Special parenchyma tissue is found in the aquatic plants and some land plants (e.g., petiole of Banana, Canna). It is known as aerenchyma. It consists of a network of parenchyma cells that enclose very large air cavities.

questions.question: The substance found in the cell wall of cork or bark that makes it impervious to water is
questions.explanation: The cork is impervious to water due to suberin deposition in the cell wall of its cells. The walls of cork cells are heavily thickened with an organic substance, suberin. Suberin makes these cells impervious to water and gases.

questions.question: The extremely thin and flat cells forming a delicate lining in the lung alveoli constitute
questions.explanation: Tongue, esophagus, and the lining of the mouth are made up of simple squamous epithelium. It is also found in blood vessels and alveoli. It protects the underlying parts of the body from mechanical injury, entry of germs, chemicals, and drying. It also forms a selectively permeable surface through which filtration occurs.

questions.question: The mineral elements found in our bone making it hard, are
questions.explanation: Bone cells are embedded in a hard matrix, which is strengthened by fibers, and hardened by calcium and phosphorus salts. The matrix is deposited in the form of concentric layers of lamellae formed around a central Haversian canal.

questions.question: Glands in our body are formed by
questions.explanation: A gland is a group of cells. Epithelial tissues are thin tissues that cover all the exposed surfaces of the body. They form the external skin, the inner lining of the mouth, digestive tract, secretory glands, the lining of hollow parts of every organ such as the heart, lungs, eyes, ears, the urogenital tract, as well as the ventricular system of the brain and central canals of the spinal cord.

questions.question: The epithelium is separated from the underlying connective tissue by
questions.explanation: Epithelial tissues are physically separated from underlying connective tissues by a basement membrane or basal lamina. The portion of an epithelial cell attached to the basement membrane is called its basal surface.

questions.question: Tendons help to connect
questions.explanation: A tendon is a fibrous connective tissue that attaches muscle to bone. Tendons may also attach muscles to structures such as the eyeball.

questions.question: Most of the metabolic functions of plants are carried out by
questions.explanation: Parenchyma carries out most of the plant's metabolism. They are "lifetime dividers" and important in healing. They also store starch, oils, and water (H2O).

questions.question: The mechanical strength and rigidity of the cell wall is due to
questions.explanation: The cell walls of sclerenchyma are greatly thickened due to the presence of lignin. Lignin is a complex polymer with high tensile strength e., it does not break easily on stretching and high compressional strength, it does not buckle easily.

questions.question: Lignified, narrow, elongated and dead cells are found in
questions.explanation: Sclerenchyma tissue is dead simple permanent tissue of the plant. The cells of sclerenchyma are closely packed without intercellular spaces, like tiles in the mosaic floor so that, it can provide the strength, rigidity, flexibility, and elasticity to the plant to withstand various strains.

questions.question: Which of the following sets includes simple permanent tissues?
questions.explanation: Two simple permanent tissue in plants are parenchyma and collenchyma while two complex permanent tissue in plants are xylem and phloem. Sclerenchyma tissue is dead simple permanent tissue of the plant.

questions.question: Rhythmic contraction and relaxation throughout life, are shown by
questions.explanation: Cardiac muscles are present in the heart. They contract and relax rapidly, rhythmically, and tirelessly. They help to pump the blood to various parts of the body.

questions.question: Those organs of the body like oral cavity, esophagus, etc., which are subjected to mechanical abrasions are lined by
questions.explanation:
A nonkeratinizing stratified squamous epithelium is found at three prominent sites in the animal body:

  1. lining the esophagus,
  2. lining the sides and floor of the oral cavity, and
  3. lining the vagina.

questions.question: Which of the following helps in repair of tissue and fills up the space inside the organ?
questions.explanation: Areolar tissues join skin to muscles, fills spaces inside organs and is found around muscles, blood vessels and nerves. Hence are not concerned with bones.

questions.question: The muscular tissue which function throughout the life continuously without fatigue is
questions.explanation: Cardiac muscles show characteristics of both smooth and striated muscles. These muscles are striated, cylindrical, branched and involuntary in nature. In addition to the light and dark bands, these muscles show densely stained cross bands called intercalated dises. These act as impulse boosters. Cardiac muscles contract and relax rapidly, rhythmically and tirelessly throughout a lifetime, from early embryonic stage until death.

questions.question: Which of the following cells is found in the cartilaginous tissue of the body?
questions.explanation: The matrix of cartilage has delicate network of collagen fibres and chondrocytes (living cells). These are present in fluid-filled spaces called lacunae. Chondrocytes multiply by mitosis and help in internal growth of cartilage.

questions.question: The dead element present in the phloem is
