questions.explanation: Wavelength is the length between two consecutive peaks. The S.I unit of wavelength is  Metres (m).

questions.question: Light is a:
questions.explanation: Light is a transverse wave because its components vibrate perpendicular to the direction of propagation.

questions.question: In compression, the density of the particles:
questions.explanation: When compression takes place in the medium, the density and pressure of the medium increase. When rarefaction takes place in the medium, density, and pressure of the medium decrease.

questions.question: Frequency of ultrasonic wave is:
questions.explanation: Sound whose frequency is more than 20000 Hz is called ultrasonic sound. Animals like dolphins, bats, rats, porpoises, etc. produce and hear an ultrasonic sound.

questions.question: SI unit of frequency is:
questions.explanation: The SI unit of frequency is the hertz (Hz), named after the German physicist Heinrich Hertz; one hertz means that an event repeats once per second.

questions.question: Stethoscope works on the principle of:
questions.explanation: Multiple reflections of sound is the reflection of sound back and forth several times between reflecting surfaces. Due to multiple reflections, the sound waves get added up, this causes an increase in the amplitude of the sound. This is the principle used in the stethoscope and concert halls.

questions.question: The audible range of the human ear is:
questions.explanation: The frequency range of human hearing is called an audible range of sound. The human ear is normally sensitive to only those vibrations whose frequency lies between 20 Hz to 20000 Hz.

questions.question: Which of the following is used in echocardiography?
questions.explanation: An echocardiogram uses high-frequency sound waves (ultrasound) to create a moving picture of your heart as it works.

questions.question: Infrasonic sound is produced by:
questions.explanation: Rhinoceroses communicate with one another by using the infrasonic sound of frequency as low as 5 Hz.

questions.question: Speed of sound is maximum in:
questions.explanation: Liquids and gases are not packed as tightly as solids. The spacing of the molecules enables sound to travel much faster through a solid than a liquid or a gas. Sound travels fastest in solids followed by liquids and then gases.

questions.question: Sound of crackers is heard during festival days, but the sound of supernova explosion in space is not heard on the surface of earth because of
questions.explanation: Sound needs medium to travel. As there is no medium in the space, it can not travel from space to earth.

questions.question: The sound can travel in air when:

options.option: There is no moisture in the atmosphere
Disturbance travel from one place to another
Particles of medium travel from one place to another
Both particles as well as disturbance travel from one place to another

questions.explanation: Disturbance travel from one place to another

questions.question: Sound waves are
questions.explanation: Sound Wave is longitudinal wave. Sound propagates because of oscillation of particles of medium parallel to the disturbance, thus sound wave is longitudinal wave.

questions.question: On a slinky we can produce
questions.explanation: A slinky is a long helical spring, usually made of steel. It is flexible and has appreciable elasticity. It produces transverse waves when one end is fixed and the other end is stretched and given a jerk at a right angle to its length. It produces longitudinal waves when compressions are given at regular intervals of time at the free end of the slinky. A disturbance that propagates through a medium is called a wave.

questions.question: The distance between consecutive crests is
questions.explanation: The distance between two consecutive crests or two consecutive troughs is called wavelength of the wave. It is represented by {tex}\lambda{/tex}.

questions.question: The distance between adjacent crests is called:
questions.explanation: The distance between two consecutive crests or two consecutive troughs is called wavelength of the wave. It is represented by {tex}\lambda {/tex}.

questions.question: Hertz is a unit of:
questions.explanation: The number of sound waves produced in unit time is called the frequency of sound waves. The SI unit of frequency is 'hertz'.

questions.question: If the density of a medium through which sound is propagation is maximum, then this point is called ________.
questions.explanation: The action when coils come closer is called a compression and when coils go farther from each other is called rarefaction. Compression is called the region of high density and pressure. Rarefaction is called the region of low density and pressure.

questions.question: If the density of a medium through which sound is propagation is minimum, then this point is called ________.
questions.explanation: The action when coils come closer is called a compression and when coils go farther from each other is called rarefaction. Compression is called the region of high density and pressure. Rarefaction is called the region of low density and pressure.

questions.question: The audible range for a normal human being is:
questions.explanation: The audible range of an average human ear lies between 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz. Humans cannot hear sounds having a frequency less than 20 Hz and greater than 20,000 Hz.

questions.question: Ultrasonic is not used in:
questions.explanation: The radio waves should not cause any interference in the generation of the sound waves. They are very far apart in frequency and ultrasonic waves are sound vs radio waves that are electromagnetic. If you have electronic circuitry detecting the reflected ultrasonic waves then the circuitry can be affected by the radio waves at most.

questions.question: For echo to take place for simple sounds, the distance between the source of the sound and the reflecting surface should be:
questions.explanation: If the obstacle is at a distance of 17 m at least, the reflected sound or the echo is heard after 0.1 seconds.

questions.question: ________ have frequencies more than 20,000 hertz.
questions.explanation: The sound of frequency more than 20,000Hz is called ultrasonics. Ultrasonic sounds have a frequency above the upper limit of human hearing.

questions.question: Two sounds of same pitch and loudness differ in their:
questions.explanation: Quality is the sensation received by the ear by which, we are able to differentiate two sounds (even if they are of the same pitch and loudness). The quality actually depends on the shape of the waveform and the number of overtones.

questions.question: Echo is not heard in a room of 10m {tex}\times{/tex} 10m {tex}\times{/tex} 10m dimension due to (speed of sound in air = 300 m/s).
questions.explanation: The persistence of sound due to the multiple reflections of sound in an auditorium is called reverberation. It can be reduced by wall proofing and roof proofing the auditorium with sound-absorbent materials like compressed fiberboard. The seat should have sound-absorbing properties.

questions.question: The instruments Megaphone, Stethoscope, Hearing acids and Soundboards work based on the principle of:
questions.explanation: The stethoscope is a medical diagnostic instrument based on multiple reflections of sound waves. Megaphone is a horn-shaped tube which is working on reflection of sound waves.

questions.question: A wave is moving with a speed of 3000 m/s with reversal after every 0.1 second. The wavelength of the wave is
questions.explanation:
Given T = 0.1 sec
V = 3000 m/s
V = v {tex}\lambda{/tex} = {tex}\lambda{/tex}/t
3000 = {tex}\lambda{/tex}/0.1
{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 300m

questions.question: Sonic booms are caused due to the variation of
questions.explanation: A sonic boom is caused by the pressure change across the shock wave. The pressure from sonic booms caused by aircraft often is a few pounds per square foot.

questions.question: The frequency of a visible light of wavelength 600 nm is (speed of light = 3  ×108 m/s), (1nm = 10-9 m)
questions.explanation:
The wavelength (λ) times frequency (ν) equals the speed of light (c), which is 3.00×108m/s to three significant figures.
c = {tex}\lambda{/tex}⋅ν
v =c / {tex}\lambda{/tex}
Frequency is measured in Hertz (Hz), which is /s or s−1.
Convert 600 nm to meters since the unit for the speed of light is m/s.
1 nm=1{tex}\times{/tex}10−9m
{tex}600\ nm\times \ \frac{1\times {{10}^{-9}}\ m}{1\ nm}\ ={/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-7m
{tex}V=\ \frac{c}{\lambda }{/tex}
{tex}V\ =\ \frac{3\times \ {{10}^{8}}\ m/s}{6\ \times {{10}^{-7}}m}\ {/tex} = 0.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 1015 = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 1014 Hz

questions.question: The terms ultrasonic, supersonic and infrasonic mean

options.option: different parameters and so cannot be related

questions.explanation: Ultrasonic waves are acoustic waves that are so high in frequency that humans can’t hear them; however, infrasonic waves are sound waves that are lower in frequency than what humans can hear. A subsonic wave is a wave that is traveling slower than the speed of sound and supersonic wave travels faster. The terms ultrasonic, supersonic, and infrasonic mean different parameters and so cannot be related.

questions.question: On a hot summer day, speed of sound was recorded at Chennai and Delhi as vc and vd then
questions.explanation:
On a hot summer day, the Temperature of  Chennai is greater than in Delhi.
Molecules at higher temperatures have more energy, thus they can vibrate faster. Since the molecules vibrate faster, sound waves can travel more quickly.
Vc > Vd

questions.question: Sound is produced when

options.option: The prongs of the fork are vibrated

questions.explanation:
Sound is produced due to the vibration of an object. The motion of objects causes vibration. Vibration is a kind of rapid to and fro motion of an object about a central position.

Example:

  • It can be produced by clapping of palms, beating a table, or drum.
  • Sound is produced by a guitar and flute because of the vibration of the guitar’s string and vibration of the air column of flute respectively.
  • The sound of the human voice is produced due to the vibration of the vocal cords.
  • A stretched rubber band, when plucked vibrates and produces sound.

questions.question: Noise and Music differ in their
questions.explanation: The sound that is produced when several frequencies are mixed is called a note. The unpleasant sounds around us are called noise. The sounds which are pleasant to hear are called all musical sound.

questions.question: Note is sound
questions.explanation: Sound of mixture of several frequencies is called note. A note is pleasant to listen.

questions.question: A key of a mechanical piano struck gently and then struck again but much harder this time. In the second case

options.option: sound will be louder but pitch will not be differ
sound will be louder but pitch will be lower
both loudness and pitch will remain unaffected
sound will be louder and pitch will also be higher

questions.explanation: Pitch will not change because loudness depends on amplitude. By hitting it hard it will make its amplitude higher. But the frequency will be same. And the point is to be noted is that the loudness depends on the amplitude not the frequency.

questions.question: In SONAR, we use
questions.explanation: SONAR stands for Sound Navigation And Ranging. This is a device that is used to measure depth of sea bed, locate scraps, wrecks, submarines of enemies, etc. in the water by producing ultrasound. It is fitted over ships and submarines. Ultrasonic sound waves are produced by transmitter of SONAR and when these waves return back to the detector, after striking the object in the sea bed, they are analyzed with the help of computer. The shape and position of objects under sea and ocean is detected on the basis of speed and nature of reflected ultrasound waves.

questions.question: Sound travels in air if

options.option: particles of medium travel from one place to another
there is no moisture in the atmosphere
both particles as well as disturbance travel from one place to another

questions.explanation: Sound travels in air if disturbance moves. As during the propagation of sound waves, particles only vibrates in its own position and disturbances created by the vibration of particle moves from one place to other.

questions.question: On which of the following factor the speed of propagation of pulse in a slinky does not depend?

questions.explanation: Oscillation will be perpendicular to the length or direction of energy transfer. Activity that carries energy from one place to another without actually moving any matter is a wave motion.

questions.explanation: More elastic and lengthier the spring will be, the motion of a pulse can be studied batter.

in a small part of the medium

questions.explanation:

questions.question: The minimum size of a room required to hear an echo of sound with a speed of 300 m/s is
questions.explanation: The total distance covered by the sound from the point of generation to the reflecting surface and back should be at least (300 m/s) {tex}\times{/tex} 0.1 s = 30 m. Thus, for hearing distinct echoes, the minimum distance of the obstacle from the source of sound must be half of this distance. i.e., 30/2 = 15 m.

questions.question: When we change feeble sound to loud sound we increase its
questions.explanation: The loudness or softness of a sound is determined basically by its amplitude. So for a loud sound it must have higher amplitude.

questions.question: Earthquake produces which kind of sound before the main shock wave begins
questions.explanation: Earthquakes produces low frequency (i.e., 5 Hz) infrasound before the main shock waves begin which possibly alert the animals and they get disturbed.

questions.question: Infrasound can be heard by
questions.explanation: The audible range of sound for human beings extends from about 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz. Sounds of frequencies below 20 Hz are called infrasonic sound or infrasound. Animals like dogs, elephants, rhinoceros, whales etc. produce and hear infrasonic sound.

questions.question: Before playing the orchestra in a musical concert, a sitarist tries to adjust the tension and pluck the string suitably. By doing so, he is adjusting

options.option: frequency of the sitar string with the frequency of other musical instruments

questions.explanation: Sitarist is adjusting frequency of the sitar string with the frequency of other musical instruments. Because if it is not done so, the sound will be unpleasant to listen.

questions.question: Reflection of sound obeys the law
questions.explanation:
Angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection.
Sound and light obey the laws of reflection  {tex}​​\angle{/tex}r = {tex}​​\angle{/tex}i.

questions.question: In which of the following medium will the sound wave travel faster
questions.explanation: Higher density and incompressibility brings higher speed for sound.

questions.question: Sound waves cannot propagate in
questions.explanation: Sound needs a material medium like solid, liquid, or gas to travel and be heard because the molecules of solid, liquid, and gases carry sound waves from one place to another. Sound cannot travel through the vacuum or empty space because the vacuum has no molecules which can vibrate and carry sound waves.

questions.question: Ramjeet, while verifying the laws of reflection of sound, measured the angled between the incident sound wave and reflected wound wave to be 130o. The angle of incidence is:
