questions.question: Curved soundboard may be placed behind the stage because:

options.option: it reflects the sound to a particular point in the hall
after reflection, sound waves spread evenly across the width of the hall.

questions.explanation: A curved soundboard may be placed behind the stage so that sound after reflecting from the soundboard spreads evenly across the width of the hall.  And regular reflection takes place.

questions.question: In an auditorium or big hall the walls are provided with sound-absorbant materials, why?

options.option: So, that echo effect may be minimized.
So, that the sound may appear to be pleasing
So, that the sound from the stage may be heard properly.

questions.explanation: The roof and walls of the auditorium are generally covered with sound-absorbent materials to reduce reverberation. These materials reduce the formation of echoes by absorbing sound waves.

questions.question: In the experiment of verification of reflection of sound, the incident sound is directed along:

options.option: at an angle of 45o from the axis of the tube
at an angle of 30o from the axis of the tube

questions.explanation: In the experiment of verification of reflection of sound, the incident sound is directed along because if it is normal to the axis of the tube then it will not form a proper angle. The angle between the incident ray and reflected ray may vary but the angle between the incident ray and normal ray have to be equal to the angle between the normal ray and the reflected ray.

questions.question: A student while verifying the laws of reflection of sound measured the angle between the incident sound wave and reflected sound wave as 110o. The angle of reflection is:
questions.explanation:
The angle of reflection of sound is always equal to angle of incidence of sound.
∠r = ∠i = 55o

questions.question: For reflection of sound wave, we need:

options.option: A large size, the opaque reflecting surface

questions.explanation: A good reflector of sound and not light. Sound waves are much longer than light waves so they require a much larger area of reflection.

questions.question: A pulse
questions.explanation: Pulse is a short duration disturbance. Pulse does not repeat. Pulse is formed in small portion and Pulse disturbance is momentary.

questions.question: The wave number of wave of wavelength 10 m is
questions.explanation: Wave number = {tex}\frac1\lambda\;=\frac1{10}{/tex}= 0.1 m-1

questions.question: Sometimes sound produced by bats, dolphins are
questions.explanation: Sound of frequencies above 20,000 Hz is known as ultrasonic which are produced by bats and dolphins.

questions.question: Wave motion transfers
questions.explanation: 'Wave' is a common term for a number of different ways in which energy is transferred: In electromagnetic waves, energy is transferred through vibrations of electric and magnetic fields. In sound waves, energy is transferred through the vibration of air particles or particles of a solid through which the sound travels.

questions.question: Speed of light or electromagnetic wave is higher than that of the speed of sound in air by
questions.explanation: The speed of sound in air is 344 m/s. The speed of light in air is about 300,000,000 meters per second = 300 {tex}\times{/tex} 106 m/s.

questions.question: The wave having an amplitude of 5 cm and frequency f = 100 Hz can be best represented by
questions.explanation: In this graph, amplitude represents on y-axis and frequency on the x-axis. The wave having an amplitude of 5 cm and frequency f = 100 Hz can be best represented by a given graph.

questions.question: Sonic booms are caused by the combination of
questions.explanation: A sonic boom is a sound associated with the shock waves created whenever an object traveling through the air travels faster than the speed of sound.  Sonic boom created by an aerospace vehicle when the vehicle is flown at supersonic speed. The pressure from sonic booms caused by aircraft often is a few pounds per square foot.

questions.question: Absorbent materials are to be used while making interior design in an auditorium as
questions.explanation: To reduce reverberation in big halls and auditoriums, the roofs and walls are covered by sound-absorbing materials like compressed fibre boards, rough plaster or draperies.

questions.question: Infrasonic fi, Audible fa and ultrasonic fu sounds are related as
questions.explanation:
Infrasound frequency is lesser than 20 Hz .
Audible sound frequency lies between 20 Hz to 20000 Hz.
Ultrasound  wave  frequency is more than 20000 Hz .

questions.question: Which of the following is not the character of mechanical waves?

options.option: Propagation depends on the elasticity of medium

questions.explanation:
• Mechanical Waves require a medium, in order to transport their energy from one place to another.
  • Mechanical Waves have two types of waves: Transverse and Longitudinal.
  • Mechanical Wave is a wave that is not capable of transmitting its energy through empty space.
  • Speed is comparatively higher is not a character of mechanical waves.

questions.question: In the bell jar experiment, as air is removed

options.option: both intensity falls and speed of sound falls

questions.explanation: As the air in the jar is being pumped out, the sound becomes fainter, although it is the same current that is passing through the bell. On reducing the air pressure further, the intensity of the sound reduces and stops.

questions.question: When sound gets reflected from a surface:

options.option: the angle of reflection is always equal to the angle of incidence
the angle of reflection is always equal to 90o
the angle of reflection is always more than the angle of incidence
the angle of reflection is always less than the angle of incidence

questions.explanation:
Laws of reflection:

  1. The angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection.
  2. The incident ray, the reflected ray and normal at the point of incidence all lie in the same plane.

questions.question: When a pulse is sent through  slinky/string, the physical quantity that travels along its length is:
questions.explanation: Wave transfers energy. Energy travels through a wave.

questions.question: The distance between two consecutive compression of a sound wave is 5 cm. Then the wavelength of the wave is equal to
questions.explanation: For a longitudinal wave (sound), the distance between two successive compressions equals one wavelength. Therefore the wavelength is 5 cm.

questions.question: In the experiment for determining the velocity of a pulse propagating along the length of string, we prefer a long thick cotton string:

options.option: So that time taken by pulse to move from one end to string to other may be accurately determined.
so that pulse may move through it easily
because cotton string is cheap and easily available
because pulse cannot be formed in a thin short string

questions.explanation: If any twists and bends in string then time taken by pulse to move from one end to other may not be accurate.

questions.question: Two audio devices of the same brand differ in their
questions.explanation: Every speaker produces certain frequencies that are louder or softer than others.

questions.question: Speed of sound in air and water are given as va and vw respectively. Then
questions.explanation: Sounds in water and sounds in the air that has the same pressures have very different intensities because the density of water is much greater than the density of air and because the speed of sound in water is much greater than the speed of sound in air.

questions.question: While verifying the laws of reflection of sound, out of following precautions which one should not be followed?

options.option: The reflecting surface should be smooth and hard
Length of pipes should be sufficiently long
Ear should not be placed close to the pipe

questions.explanation: Energy may be lost. To avoid the diffraction of sound and make its flow unidirectional long tube is used. The small diameter of the tube allows the selected sound wave to travel clearly.

questions.question: The frequency of a source is 20 kHz. The frequencies of the sound waves produced by it in water and air will

options.option: Be the same as that of the source
Depend upon the density of the media.
Depend upon the velocity of the waves in these media
Depend upon the wavelength of the waves in these media

questions.explanation: Frequency of the sound waves is independent of the medium. It will be same in water as well as in air.

questions.question:
In an experiment to determine the speed of sound, a gun was fired and an observer at a distance away measured the time interval between seeing the flash of the gun and hearing the shot. Which of the following will not affect the result?

  1. The frequency of the gunshot.
  2. The temperature of the environment.
  3. The loudness of the gunshot.
questions.explanation: (i) and (iii) only

questions.question:
Statement 1: Echo is produced when sound is incident on hard and polished surface.
Statement 2: Sound energy can be totally reflected by the objects with soft and loose texture.

options.option: Statement 1 is true but statement 2 is false.
Both statements 1 and 2 are true but statement 2 is not the correct explanation of statement 1.
Both statements 1 and 2 are true and statement 2 is the correct explanation of statement 1.

questions.explanation: Sound energy gets partially absorbed by objects with soft and loose texture.

questions.question: A student plotted the following four graphs representing the variation of velocity v of sound in a gas with the pressure P for a given temperature. Which one is correct?

A sound wave travelling in a medium is represented as shown in figure.

If vibrating source of sound makes 360 oscillations in 2 minutes, then the amplitude, wavelength ({tex}\lambda{/tex}) and frequency ({tex}\nu{/tex}) of the sound wave respectively are (Take velocity of sound as 342 m s-1)
questions.explanation:
Here, amplitude of sound wave = 1 m (given)
Wavelength of sound wave, {tex}\lambda{/tex} = {tex}\frac{v_s}{v}{/tex} ...(i)
In 1 second, number of oscillations = {tex}\frac{360}{2 \times 60}{/tex} = 3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Frequency of sound wave, v = 3 Hz
Put the value of v in (i),
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Wavelength of sound wave, {tex}\lambda{/tex} = {tex}\frac{342}{3}{/tex} = 114 m

questions.question: A submarine emits a SONAR pulse which returns from an under water cliff in 1.05 second. If the speed of sound in salt water is 1531 m s-1, how far away is the cliff?
questions.explanation:
Time taken = 1.05 s
Speed of sound in salt water = 1531 m s-1
Distance travelled by pulse from submarine to the cliff and back to submarine,
2 {tex}\times{/tex} Distance = 1531 {tex}\times{/tex} 1.05
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Distance between cliff and submarine,
S = {tex}\frac{1531 \times 1.05}{2}{/tex}= 803.8 m

questions.question: Sound travels with a speed of about 330 m s-1.What is the wavelength of sound whose frequency is 660 Hz?
questions.explanation:
Speed of sound, vs = 330 m s-1
Frequency of sound, u = 660 Hz
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Wavelength of sound,{tex}\lambda{/tex} = {tex}\frac{v_s}{v}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{330}{660}{/tex} = {tex}\frac 12{/tex} = 0.5 m

questions.question: The diagram represents two different sound waves.

options.option: The frequency of is less than Y but its pitch is same as Y.
The frequency of X is same as Y but its pitch is higher than Y.
The frequency and pitch of X is same as the frequency and pitch of Y.
The frequency and pitch of X is less than that of Y of X is less than y.

questions.explanation: The frequency of wave X is lower than wave Y, hence its pitch is lower than wave Y too. The pitch of sound depends on the frequency.

questions.question: An insect makes sound that is higher than the maximum audible frequency of human beings. Given that speed of sound in air is approximately 340 m s-1, which of the following could be the wavelength of the sound?
questions.explanation:
v = {tex}v \lambda {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} \lambda{/tex} = {tex}\frac{v}{\nu}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{340}{20,000}{/tex} = 0.017m = 1.7 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The only possible wavelength is 1.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 m

questions.question:
A microphone is connected to a cathode ray oscilloscope. A loudspeaker giving a single note is switch on. The trace shown in the figure appears on the screen.

Which of the following shows the softer but same frequency note of the given note?
questions.explanation: The sound wave having less amplitude will be softer.

questions.question:
The following figure shows the displacement time graph for a sound wave produced by an instrument.

Which graph can be obtained when another musical instrument produces a note of the same pitch but of a different quality?

A man who is standing at a point between two parallel walls fires a pistol. He hears echoes after 0.5 s and 0.8 s. A further echo is heard, after firing, at a time of ________.
(Speed of sound = 330 m s-1)
questions.explanation:
Time taken by the sound produced by man to reflect back from first wall = 0.5 s.
Time taken by sound produced by man to reflect back from second wall = 0.8 s
A further echo is heard when sound reflected from first wall suffer one more reflection from second wall.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The further echo is heard at a time of (0.5 + 0.8) s or 1.3 s.

questions.question: A stone is dropped from the top of a tower of 125 m high into a pond which is at base of the tower. When will the splash be heard at the top? (Given g = 10 m s-2 and speed of sound = 340 m s-1.)
questions.explanation:
Initial velocity, u = 0 (body at rest)
Let t1 be the time taken by the stone to reach the pond.
For a freely falling body
h = ut + {tex}\frac 12{/tex}gt2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 125 = 0 {tex}\times{/tex} t1 + {tex}\frac 12{/tex}10 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}t_1^2 {/tex}
{tex}t_1^2 {/tex} = 25 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t1 = 5 s
Let the time taken by sound to reach the top = t2
Speed = {tex}\frac{\text { Distance }}{\text { Time }}{/tex} or 340 = {tex}\frac{125}{t_2} {/tex} or t2 = {tex}\frac{125}{340} {/tex} = 0.36 s
Time after which splash will be heard at the top
= t1 + t2 = 5 + 0.36 = 5.36 s

chapters.name: Why Do We Fall ill (FA Only)

questions.question: Which one of the following is not important for individual health?

options.option: Living in a large and well-furnished house

questions.explanation: Health is a state of being well enough to function well physically, mentally, and socially. It depends upon the surroundings or the environment. Living in a clean environment under good economic conditions, and social equality and harmony are important for individual health. Good economic conditions are needed so that an individual can work and earn, and get food for nutrition. Living in a large and well-furnished house is not important for individual health.

questions.question: 'Penicillin', a life-saving antibiotic, was discovered by:
questions.explanation: 'Penicillin' is an antibiotic. It was discovered by Alexander Fleming. It is one of the first, and still one of the most widely used antibiotic agents, derived from the Penicillium mold. It works by indirectly bursting bacterial cell walls. Edward Jenner is credited with the discovering of vaccination.

questions.question: Making anti-viral drugs is more difficult than making anti-bacterial medicines because:

options.option: Viruses are on the borderline of living and non-living.
Viruses have very few biochemical mechanisms of their own.

questions.explanation: A drug is an organic molecule that activates or inhibits the function of a biomolecule such as a protein, which in turn results in a therapeutic benefit to the patient. Making anti-viral drugs is more difficult than making anti-bacterial medicines because viruses do not use the biochemical pathways that can be targeted with drugs. All viruses live inside the host cells, whereas bacteria rarely do so. Antibiotics do not function against viral infections.

questions.question: Which one of the following is deficiency disease?
questions.explanation: Goiter (swelling in the neck resulting from an enlarged thyroid gland) is a deficiency disease, caused by the deficiency of iodine. Diabetes is a group of metabolic disorders in which a person has high blood sugar levels over a prolonged period. Malaria is a mosquito-borne infectious disease. Tetanus is an infection caused by Clostridium tetani.

questions.question: The chemical that kills or stop the growth of certain kinds of microbes are called:
questions.explanation: Antibiotics are the chemicals that kill or stop the growth of certain kinds of microbes. Vaccines are biological preparations that provide active acquired immunity to a particular disease. Microbes or microscopic organisms are tiny creatures that are too small to be seen with the unaided eye. E.g. Fungi.

questions.question: Rabies occurs through the bite of:
questions.explanation: Rabies is caused by the bite of a rabid dog. Malaria may occur through the bite of a female Anopheles mosquito. A female Culex mosquito is a vector of Filariasis or Encephalitis.

questions.question: AIDS is caused by:
questions.explanation: Acquired Immuno-Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS) is caused by a virus (Human Immuno-deficiency Virus). AIDS is a spectrum of conditions caused by infection with a human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). As the infection progresses, it interferes with the immune system, increasing the risk of common infections like tuberculosis, as well as other opportunistic infections.

questions.question: If you live in an overcrowded and poorly ventilated house, it is possible that you may suffer from which of the following diseases:
questions.explanation: Airborne diseases are diseases that are caused by pathogens that are transmitted through the air. Socioeconomics and living conditions are an important factor in the transmission of these diseases.

questions.question: The symptom of goitre is:
questions.explanation: The swelling of the thyroid gland is a symptom of goiter. The following are the most common symptoms that are seen with goiter: throat symptoms of tightness, cough, and hoarseness. trouble swallowing (dysphagia) in severe cases, difficulty breathing (possibly with a high-pitch sound).

questions.question: Which one of the following does not help in the prevention of AIDS?

options.option: Using the same syringe again and again

questions.explanation: Using the same syringe, again and again, does not help in the prevention of AIDS. The use of disposable syringes helps in the prevention of AIDS.

questions.question: You are aware of Polio Eradication Programme in your city. Children are vaccinated because:

options.option: Prevents the entry of polio causing micro-organisms.

questions.explanation: Poliomyelitis, often called polio or infantile paralysis, is an infectious disease caused by the polio virus. Polio vaccination creates immunity in the body against polio virus and prevent poliomyelitis (polio). The vaccine produces antibodies in the blood.

questions.question: 'BCG' vaccine is given to infants for protection against:
questions.explanation: Bacillus Calmette-Guérin (BCG) vaccine is a vaccine primarily used against Tuberculosis. It is given to infants for protection against Tuberculosis (TB). TB is a serious infection that affects the lungs and sometimes other parts of the body, such as the bones, joints, and kidneys. It can also cause meningitis.

questions.question: Which one is not sexually transmitted disease?
questions.explanation: Diabetes is not a sexually transmitted disease. Diabetes is a group of metabolic disorders in which the affected person has high blood sugar levels over a prolonged period.

questions.question: Which one of the following causes kala-azar?
questions.explanation:
Leishmania is a genus of trypanosomes that are responsible for the disease leishmaniasis. Visceral leishmaniasis, also known as kala-azar, black fever, and Dumdum fever, is the most severe form of leishmaniasis. Kala azar is the second largest parasitic killer in the world.

Ascaris is a genus of parasitic nematode worms known as the "small intestinal roundworms", which is a type of parasitic worm.

questions.question: Which of the following is not transferred by houseflies?
questions.explanation: AIDS

questions.question: Which of the following disease is caused by bacteria?
questions.explanation: A number of diseases are caused by bacteria that include Diarrhoea, Typhoid, Tuberculosis etc. Typhoid is a bacterial infection caused by salmonellla typhi. A type of bacteria called mycobacterium tuberculosis causes Tuberculosis.

questions.question: ________ is the commonest carrier of pathogens.
questions.explanation: The most common carrier of the pathogen is housefly. The house fly is a carrier of a large number of pathogens, which can cause serious infections and diseases in humans as well as in animals.

questions.question: Which of the following is non-communicable disease?
questions.explanation: Non-communicable diseases are not caused by any infectious agent. They are restricted only to those persons who are suffering from them. So, Allergy is a non-communicable disease.

questions.question: Which of the following disease is transmitted by animal bite?
questions.explanation: Rabies is spread by the bite of infected dog and other animals. It is a viral disease caused by a rabies virus that is present in the saliva of infected animals.

questions.question: Which of the following disease is viral disease?
questions.explanation: Polio is a highly contagious disease caused by a virus that attacks the nervous system. Children younger than 5 years old are more likely to contract the virus than any other group.

questions.question: Which of the following is sexually transmitted disease?
questions.explanation: Sexually Transmitted diseases are transmitted by sexual contact from one partner to another. However, these diseases are not transmitted by causal physical contact. Syphilis, AIDS, and Gonorrhea all are Sexually Transmitted diseases.

questions.question: After vaccination, the body build up
questions.explanation: After vaccination, the body learns to fight with that microbe. And the body builds up antibodies.

questions.question: Providing living conditions that are not crowded, can help to prevent
questions.explanation: In Air-borne diseases microbial agents can move from an affected person to someone else through air. So, providing living conditions that are not crowded, can help to prevent Air-borne diseases .

questions.question: The antibiotic penicillin kills the bacteria by blocking the synthesis of
questions.explanation: Antibiotic is given to treat or prevent bacterial infection. Some antibiotics damage the cell wall of bacteria and thus kill the bacteria.

questions.question: Which of the following sets of diseases can spread in the absence of supplies of safe drinking water?
questions.explanation: The Cholera and Jaundice infected bacteria will enter new hosts through the water they drink and cause disease.

questions.question: The severity of the disease manifestation depends on

options.option: the number of microbes causing the disease.

questions.explanation: The severity of the disease depends on the organ or part of the body infected, age of an infected person, number of microbes causing the disease, and the signs and symptoms. For example, Malaria causing protozoans enters the human body through a mosquito, affects the liver and red blood cells.

questions.question: We can fool our immune system into developing memory for a particular infection by

options.option: living in places that are not overcrowded

questions.explanation: We fool the immune system of the human body into developing memory for particular infection by putting something that mimics the microbes we want to vaccinate against, into the body. This does not actually cause the disease but this would prevent any subsequent exposure to the infecting microbe from turning into actual disease.

questions.question: Sleeping sickness is caused by ______
questions.explanation: Sleeping Sickness or African trypanosomiasis is a parasitic disease that is caused by the parasite named Trypanosoma Brucie. It spreads through the bite of a fly called Tsetse which injects this disease into the human body.

questions.question: Pneumonia is spread by the air-borne ________.
questions.explanation: Air-borne diseases: Microbial agents can move from an affected person to someone else through the air. It occurs through the little droplets thrown out by an infected person who sneezes or coughs. Someone standing close by can breathe in these droplets, and microbes get a chance to start a new infection. For example, Common cold, pneumonia, tuberculosis.

questions.question: Liver is damaged by the virus in the disease ________.
questions.explanation: Jaundice is a viral disease, that causes damage to the liver.

questions.question: ________ diseases affect the general health of a person.
questions.explanation: A disease that lasts for many years is called chronic disease. Some of the chronic diseases may last for the entire life.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a viral disease?
questions.explanation: Typhoid, fever, cholera, tuberculosis, anthrax are causes by Bacteria. Common cold, influenza, dengue, fever, AIDS causes by viruses.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a bacterial disease?
questions.explanation: Typhoid, fever, cholera, tuberculosis, anthrax are causes by Bacteria. Common cold, influenza, dengue, fever, AIDS are causes by viruses

questions.question: Which one of the following disease is not transmitted by mosquito ?
questions.explanation: Typhoid is caused by the Salmonella typhi and paratyphi bacteria and spread via faecal oral contamination.

questions.question: Which one of the following disease is not caused by bacteria?
questions.explanation: Typhoid, cholera, tuberculosis, anthrax are caused by Bacteria. Malaria is a very common disease in human beings, which is caused by a protozoan parasite.

questions.question: Which one of the following disease is caused by protozoans?
questions.explanation: Malaria is a very common disease in human beings, which is caused by a protozoan parasite called Plasmodium vivax. Malaria is spread by the bite of an infected female Anopheles mosquito which is the vector.

questions.question: Which one of the following has a long term effect on the health of an individual?
questions.explanation: Tobacco contains a drug called nicotine. A drug is anything you put into your body that makes it work differently. Tobacco also contains specific harmful substances or chemicals. Most smokers would quit if they could, but they are addicted.

questions.question: Obtaining fish from natural water resources is known as:
questions.explanation: Capture fishing: It is the process of obtaining fish from natural resources.

questions.question: The commonest antimalarial drug is obtained from
questions.explanation: Quinine, as a component of the bark of the cinchona (Quina-Quina) tree, was used to treat malaria from as early as the 1600s, when it was referred to as the "Jesuits' bark," "cardinal's bark," or "sacred bark."

questions.question: Vaccine is not available against which of the following diseases?
questions.explanation: There is no licensed vaccine against any human parasitic disease and Plasmodium falciparum malaria, a major cause of infectious mortality, presents a great challenge to vaccine developers.

questions.question: Which of the following can make you ill if you come in contact with an infected person ?
questions.explanation: Air-borne diseases occurs through the little droplets thrown out by an infected person who sneezes or coughs.

questions.question: AIDS cannot be transmitted by
questions.explanation: Most commonly, people get or transmit HIV/AIDS through sexual behaviors and needle or syringe use. Only certain body fluids—blood, semen, pre-seminal fluid, rectal fluids, vaginal fluids, and breast milk—from a person who has HIV can transmit HIV. AIDS cannot be transmitted by hugs.

questions.question: Making anti-viral drugs is more difficult than making anti-bacterial medicines because

options.option: viruses are on the border line of living and non-living
viruses make use of host machinery
viruses have very few biochemical mechanisms of their own

questions.explanation: Antibiotics commonly block biochemical pathways important for bacteria. These kill the bacteria by inhibiting its growth. But the same antibiotics do not work on viruses. Because viruses do not use biochemical pathways such as bacteria and they have few biochemical mechanisms of their own.

questions.question: Which one of the following cause kala-azar?
questions.explanation: Kala-azar is caused by bites from female phlebotomine sandflies – the vector (or transmitter) of the leishmania parasite.

questions.question: If you live in a overcrowded and poorly ventilated house, it is possible that you may suffer from which of the following diseases
questions.explanation: Someone standing close by can breathe in these droplets, and microbes get chance to start a new infection. In Air-borne diseases microbial agents can move from an affected person to someone else through air. If you live in a overcrowded and poorly ventilated house, it is possible that you may suffer Air-borne diseases.

questions.question: Which diseases is not transmitted by mosquitoes ?
questions.explanation: We should not allow water to stagnate in and around our house to prevent mosquitoes from breeding in the stagnated water. Once they hatch, they can cause various diseases like malaria, dengue, Brain fever or encephalitis, etc. Pneumonia is not transmitted by mosquitoes. Most pneumonia is caused by either bacterial or viral organisms.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not important for individual health ?

options.option: Living in a large and well furnished house

questions.explanation: The garbage thrown in open places, overflowing drains or sewer water, stagnant water, etc., are the places where disease-causing microbes multiply and mosquitoes and flies breed. These mosquitoes and flies act as carriers of disease-causing microbes. As a result, diseases may spread in the community and affect individual health. Thus, public cleanliness is important for individual health. Living in a large and well furnished house is not important for individual health.

questions.question: We should not allow mosquitoes to breed in our surroundings because they

options.option: multiply very fast and cause pollution

questions.explanation: We should not allow water to stagnate in and around our house to prevent mosquitoes from breeding in the stagnated water. Once they hatch, they can cause various diseases like malaria, dengue, etc.

questions.question: Viruses, which cause hepatitis, are transmitted through
questions.explanation: Hepatitis A is caused by an infection with the hepatitis A virus (HAV). This type of hepatitis is most commonly transmitted by consuming food or water contaminated by feces from a person infected with hepatitis A.

questions.question: Vectors can be define as

options.option: animals carry the infecting agents from sick person to another healthy person

questions.explanation: Some animals work as vehicles to transfer infection from one person to another. Such animals are called vectors.

questions.question: Which among the following diseases spreads through sexual contact as well as through blood?
questions.explanation: Most commonly, people get or transmit HIV/AIDS through sexual behaviors and needle or syringe use. Only certain body fluids - blood, semen, pre-seminal fluid, rectal fluids, vaginal fluids, and breast milk - from a person who has HIV can transmit HIV.

questions.question: The set of diseases which spread through sexual contacts as well as through placenta to the foetus are
questions.explanation: Syphilis and AIDS are a chronic bacterial disease that is contracted chiefly by infection during sexual intercourse, but also congenitally by infection.

questions.question: The diseases where microbes are the immediate causes, are called
questions.explanation: When a disease happens because of a microorganism, it is called infectious disease. Bacteria, protozoa, virus and fungi are the causes of diseases in this case. Example: diarrhea, tuberculosis, dengue, malaria, hepatitis, etc.

questions.question: Identify the set of diseases which spread through little droplets that are let in the air by infected persons.
questions.explanation: Many common infections can spread by airborne transmission at least in some cases, including Anthrax (inhalational), Chickenpox, Pneumonia, Influenza, Measles, Smallpox, Cryptococcosis, and Tuberculosis.

questions.question: Immunization / Vaccination started with
questions.explanation: Edward Jenner, who discovered that it is possible to vaccinate against Small Pox using material from Cow Pox, is rightly the man who started the science of immunology.

questions.question: Malarial parasite after entering the human body reaches the
questions.explanation: Man is the intermediate host for malaria, wherein the asexual phase of the life cycle occurs. The sporozoites inoculated by the infested mosquito initiate this phase of the cycle from the liver, and the latter part continues within the red blood cells, which results in the various clinical manifestations of the disease.

questions.question: Cells of our body involved in fighting against infections, are
questions.explanation: White blood cells are the defenders of the body. Also called leukocytes, these blood components protect against infectious agents (bacteria and viruses), cancerous cells, and foreign matter.

questions.question:
Identify the wrong-matched pair from among the following:

  1. Filariasis - Protozoa
  2. Kala azar - Protozoa
  3. Hepatitis - Virus
  4. Cholera - Bacteria
questions.explanation: Lymphatic filariasis, also known as elephantiasis, is a human disease caused by parasitic worms known as filarial worms.

questions.question: Ascaris Lumbricoides causes damage to our
questions.explanation: Ascaris lumbricoides, an intestinal roundworm, is one of the most common helminthic human infections worldwide.

questions.question: When a diseases lasts for a long time and damages some part of the body, it is called a/an
questions.explanation: A disease that lasts for many years is called chronic disease. Some of the chronic diseases may last for the entire life. Chronic disease often results in debility. Example: arthritis, tuberculosis, diabetes, hypertension, etc.

questions.question: The process in which the active immune system employs many cells to the affected tissue, is called
questions.explanation: Inflammation is the immune response of the body. It is an attempt by the body to remove harmful stimuli and start the curing process. Inflammation is mostly localised reaction which results in the redness of the area, releases heat and sometimes causes irritation. In chronic cases inflammation also causes swelling. Inflammation can be acute or chronic in nature.

questions.question: Repeated exposure to a pathogen leads to the development of
questions.explanation: Whenever a pathogen enters our body, our immune system fights against that. We suffer from an infectious disease only when the microbes overpower our immune system. So, strengthening the immune system can help in preventing many diseases. Repeated exposure to a pathogen leads to the development of immunity.

questions.question: Antibiotics used in treatment of bacterial and fungal diseases are
questions.explanation: A substance that stops the growth of bacteria or kills the bacteria is called an antibiotic. Antibiotic is given to treat or prevent bacterial infection.

questions.question: In which of the following cases do you think the long term ill-effects on your health are likely to be most unpleasant?
questions.explanation: In case of jaundice as liver does not functions properly so it will have drastic long term effect on the body.

questions.question: A child who has had an infection of chicken pox or measles, does not suffer from the disease for the second time, because

options.option: the boy is advised to take vitamin tables daily after the disease.
the body has developed immense strength to fight the infection.
the immune system keeps a memory of the specific disease and responds by killing the infectious agent.
the child is given clean and nutritious food after the disease.

questions.explanation: Chickenpox, also known as varicella, is a highly contagious disease caused by the initial infection with varicella-zoster virus (VZV). The disease results in a characteristic skin rash that forms small, itchy blisters, which eventually scab over. A child who has had an infection of chickenpox or measles does not suffer from the disease for the second time, because of the immune system keeps a memory of the specific disease and responds by killing the infectious agent, in the second exposure.

questions.question: Which macro-nutrient is required in the largest quantity by the plant?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen is required in maximum amount as nitrogen is an essential element for all living organisms which is needed to make amino acids, Protein, DNA, and chlorophyll.

questions.question: 'Blue Revolution' refers to an increase in:-
questions.explanation: As the blue revolution is the management of water resources that can steer humanity to achieve drinking water and crop irrigation security. It refers to all active culturing of aquatic animals and oceans scattered the light scattered by the atmosphere make earth prevailingly blue.

questions.question: Which one of the following includes only Micronutrients:-
questions.explanation: As iron is required in very small quantity which is the main part of chlorophyll, copper activates some enzymes in plants and manganese is the second greatest micro-nutrient after iron which plays a significant role in photosynthesis.

questions.question: The enrichment of water bodies with nutrients leading to excessive growth of phytoplankton is known as:-
questions.explanation:
The enrichment of water bodies with nutrients leading to excessive growth of phytoplankton is called Eutrophication. Eutrophication or more precisely hypertrophication, is the enrichment of a water body with nutrients, usually with an excess amount of nutrients. This process induces the growth of plants and algae and due to the biomass load, may result in oxygen depletion of the water body.
Eutrophication is an enrichment of water by nutrient salts that causes structural changes to the ecosystem such as increased production of algae and aquatic plants, depletion of fish species, general deterioration of water quality, and other effects that reduce and preclude use. Eutrophication is almost always induced by the discharge of phosphate-containing detergents, fertilizers, or sewage into an aquatic system.

questions.question: Weeds affect the crop plants by -

options.option: Killing of plants in the field before they grow.

questions.explanation: The growth of weeds in the fields is harmful because they compete with other crops for nutrients, water, space, light.  A weed is a plant considered undesirable in a particular situation, "a plant in the wrong place". A plant that is a weed in one context is not a weed when growing in a situation where it is in fact wanted, and where one species of plant is a valuable crop plant, another species in the same genus might be a serious weed. They compete with the desired plants for the resources that a plant typically needs, namely, direct sunlight, soil nutrients, water, and space for growth, providing hosts and vectors for plant pathogens giving them greater opportunity to infect and degrade the quality of the desired plants.

questions.question: 'Operation Flood' in India refers to:-

options.option: Increase in milk production by the development of co-operative dairy on a large scale.

questions.explanation: Operation Flood was a rural development program started by India's National Dairy Development Board (NDDB) in 1970. It resulted in making India one of the largest producers of milk and milk products and hence is also called the White Revolution of India.

questions.question: Using fertilizers in farming is an example of
questions.explanation: High cost production

questions.question: Which of one of the following nutrients is not available in fertilisers.
questions.explanation: As iron is a micronutrient that is required mainly for enzyme activity and fertilisers are supplied mainly for replenishing macronutrients which help in plant growth.

questions.question: The science of growing vegetables, fruits and ornamental plants is called-
questions.explanation: The horticulture is the branch that deals with art, science, technology, and business of growing plants.

questions.question: Who is known as the 'Father of white revolution' in India?
questions.explanation: Dr.V. Kurein is called as the 'Father of white revolution' in India because of his initiatives and immense contribution to the dairy sector.

questions.question: Poultry fowl are susceptible to the following pathogens -
questions.explanation: all the pathogens affect the poultry. Pathogens, disease-causing microorganisms including fungi, bacteria and protozoa, are associated with increased veterinary costs, poor flock uniformity, increasing concomitant diseases, reduction in profitability and ultimately with risks to animal welfare and human health. Pathogens can be easily introduced to a poultry flock via air, pests, people, water and feed. Their prevalence and impact is largely dependent upon the quality of the environment and the health and welfare of the birds. Healthy birds have a higher tolerance to performance-limiting pathogens than stressed animals. Vectors for pathogen transfer can be mechanical and biological

questions.question: Pulses crops are rich in
questions.explanation: Pulses are a low-fat source of protein with high levels of protein and fiber. Pulses also contain important vitamins and minerals like iron, potassium, and folate. The amount of protein in beans, lentils, chickpeas, and peas is 2-3 times the levels found in cereal grains like wheat, rice, quinoa, oats, barley, and corn. Pulses, seeds, and nuts are all valuable sources of protein as well as being low in saturated fat, sodium, and also cholesterol-free. They are also good sources of fiber, complex carbohydrates, vitamins and minerals including thiamine (B1), riboflavin (B2), niacin (B3), folate, calcium, potassium, iron, and phosphorus.

questions.question: Which of the following is the high milk yielding variety of a cow?
questions.explanation:
Holstein Friesians is a breed of dairy cattle originating from the Dutch provinces of North Holland and Friesland, and Schleswig-Holstein in Northern Germany and Jutland. They are known as the world's highest-production dairy animals.
On an average Holstein Friesian (HF) cow gives 10,000-12,000 litres of milk in a 10-month lactation cycle, whereas the yields from a desi cow are only 3,000-3,600 litres.

questions.question: Murrah, Surti and Mehsana are different breeds of
questions.explanation:
1. Murrah is the most important breed of buffaloes whose home is Rohtak, Hisar, and Jind of Haryana and Nabha and Patiala districts of Punjab.
  2. Surti: The breeding tract of this breed is the Kaira and Baroda district of Gujarat. Coat colour varies from rusty brown to silver-grey. The skin is black or brown.
  3. Mehsana is a dairy breed of buffalo found in Mehsana town in Gujarat and adjoining Maharastra state.

questions.question: The unwanted plants are known as:
questions.explanation: Unwanted plants that grow together with the main crops are called weeds. The process of removing/disposing of these weeds is said to be weeding. E.g Xanthium, Parthenium. Weeds grow along with the crops competing with them for light and good nutrients. Due to this, the main crop plants get fewer nutrients, light, and space for their growth.

questions.question: Nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium are examples of
questions.explanation: Macro-nutrients

questions.question:
Poultry farming is undertaken to raise the following:

  1. Feather production
  2. Egg production
  3. Milk Production
  4. Chicken production
questions.explanation: Poultry farming is the process of raising domesticated birds such as chickens, ducks, and turkeys for the purpose of farming meat or eggs for food. Poultry is farmed in great numbers with chickens being the most numerous. More than 50 billion chickens are raised annually as a source of food, for both their meat and their eggs. Chickens raised for eggs are usually called layers while chickens raised for meat are often called broilers.

questions.question: The technique of growing two or more different crops together in the same field is known as
questions.explanation: Mixed cropping also is known as polyculture, intercropping or co-cultivation is a type of agriculture that involves planting two or more plants simultaneously in the same field, interdigitating the crop so that they grow together.

questions.question: Which of the following is not the advantage of intercropping?

options.option: Farmer can apply pesticides as per need of the crop.
Farmers can harvest and thresh both crops separately
Farmer cannot apply fertilizer as per need of the individual crop

questions.explanation:
Advantages of using intercropping are as following:

  1. It helps to maintain soil fertility.
  2. It increases productivity per unit area.
  3. It saves labour and time.
  4. Both crops can be easily harvested and processed separately.  

The farmer cannot apply fertilizer as per the need of the individual crop is not an advantage of using intercropping.

questions.question: Rotation of crops help in ______
questions.explanation:
Advantages of using crop rotation are as following:

  1. It improves soil fertility.
  2. It avoids depletion of a particular nutrient from the soil.
  3. It minimizes pest infestation and diseases.
  4. It helps in weed control.
  5. It prevents change in the chemical nature of the soil.

questions.question: Which of the following is an incorrect match?
questions.explanation: Sharbati Sonora refers to a variety of wheat. Ganga 5 improved the variety of  Maize. Pusa 24 is a verity of Soyabean. Vikram is not a type of rice.

questions.question: Rhizobium is present in ________
questions.explanation: Legume root-nodules, nitrogen fixation, and Rhizobium Soluble nitrogen is one of the largest and commonest soil nutrient deficiencies.

questions.question: Which of the following have been artificially selected?
questions.explanation: Humans have been a powerful agent in modifying wild species to suit their own requirement throughout ages by using artificial selection . From wild cabbage many varieties like broccoli, cauliflower, red cabbage, kale, cabbage and kohlrabi were obtained.

questions.question: Milk does not provide _______
questions.explanation: Including milk in your daily diet enables you to increase your intake of calcium, which you need for your bones and teeth. While milk contains a trace amount of iron, it does not contain enough for it to be your only source of this nutrient in your diet.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a draught animal?
questions.explanation: Draught animals are used in agriculture and transportation. Camel, elephant, and horse all are used for agriculture and transportation whereas sheep are not used for agriculture and transportation.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not an exotic breed of cow?
questions.explanation: Jersey, Brown Swiss, Holstein-Friesian all are Exotic breeds of cow. Sahiwal, Gir, Red Sindhi, Tharparkar are Indigenous breeds and Karan Swiss, Karan Fries, Frieswal are Cross-breeds.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a leguminous green fodder commonly available in winter?
questions.explanation: There are certain fodder crops like berseem, lucerne, turnip, etc. which, can be grown with adequate moisture while in others the production potential can be realized fully with irrigation. Lucerne is adapted to relatively dry conditions and it may tolerate heat as well as cold. The best sowing time of the crop is mid-October to early November.

questions.question: Which one of the following yields maximum milk/year?
questions.explanation: Holstein Friesians are a breed of dairy cattle originating from the Dutch provinces of North Holland and Friesland, and Schleswig-Holstein in Northern Germany and Jutland. They are known as the world's highest-production dairy animals.

questions.question: What is the pulse rate of buffalo/minute?
questions.explanation: The pulse of cattle is taken at a point on the underside of the base of the tail, the normal rate is 40 - 80 per minute in the adult. In buffalo, the pulse rate is 40 - 60 per minute.

questions.question: The poultry birds groomed for obtaining meat are called _______
questions.explanation: An egg-laying poultry bird is called hen (layers) and the poultry birds groomed for obtaining meat are called chicken or broilers.

questions.question: Which of the following poultry bird lays a maximum number of eggs annually?
questions.explanation: HH-260 is the poultry bird that lays maximum number of eggs manually. HH-260 lays up to 260 eggs annually. HH -260 is a promising indigenous hybrid of white Leghorn. This hybrid bird consumes just 110 g per day of feed for 260 eggs.

questions.question: Which of the following is the fastest growing carp?
questions.explanation: Catla is the fastest growing Indian major carp species and widely distributed throughout India, Nepal, Pakistan, Burma, and Bangladesh.

questions.question: Which of the following is micro–nutrient?
questions.explanation: Boron is an essential micronutrient which means it is essential for plant growth and development but is required in very small quantities.

questions.question: Which of the following is soil borne disease?
questions.explanation: Soil-borne disease are caused by fungal pathogens which persist (survive) in the soil matrix and in residues on the soil surface are defined as soil-borne diseases. Smut of bajra is a soil-borne disease.

questions.question: Gundhy bug is a pest of ______
questions.explanation: Gundhi bugs are also called stink bugs as a characteristic foul odor is produced by scent glands present on the abdomen. These stink bugs are distributed in all the tropical and sub-tropical regions. In India, it is used as a pest of all rice-growing areas.

questions.question: The principal cereal crop in India is _______.
questions.explanation: Rice is one of the chief grains of India. India has the largest area under rice cultivation, as it is one of the principal food crops.

questions.question: Which one of the following is broad leaf weed?
questions.explanation: Amaranthus, Chenopodium, Convolvulus are all broad-leaf weed.

questions.question: Which of the following is natural insecticide?
questions.explanation: Nicotine, Neem, and Pyrethrum are all "natural insecticide". Natural insecticides are obtained from living organisms (plants).

questions.question: Inland fishery refers to
questions.explanation: Inland reservoirs for fish include freshwater resources and brackish water resources. However, capture fishing does not give a high yield in these reservoirs. Hence, fish farming is practiced in these reservoirs.

questions.question: Kharif season extends from
questions.explanation: Kharif crops are the crops grown in rainy season extending from June to October hence are also called as monsoon crops. These crops are totally rain dependent crops. Kharif crops include paddy, soybean, sugarcane.

questions.question: Which verity of honeybee use commercial production of honey?
questions.explanation: The bee variety that is commonly used for commercial honey production is the Apis mellifera which is called the European honey bee or the western honey bee.

questions.question: The process of crossing genetically dissimilar plants of a species is called
questions.explanation: When the crossing is done between different varieties of plants, it is called intervarietal. In this case, plants of the same species but from different populations are cross-bred to produce a new variety.

questions.question: Bee pasturage refers to

options.option: the worker bees in a hive, who collect honey
the flowers available for nectar collection by the bees
the trees where bees make the hives
the hives where honeybees live and deposit honey

questions.explanation: Pasturage is the availability of flowers to the bees for nectar and pollen collection. Pasturage is related to honey production because it determines the taste of honey and the quantity of honey.

questions.question: The system of culturing five or six species of fish with different food habits, in a fishpond, is called
questions.explanation: In Composite Fish Culture System, a combination of five or six fish species is used in a single pond. The fish selected in this system belong show different food habits. This ensures no competition for food and ensures better yield.

questions.question: Manures are used in sandy soils mainly to
questions.explanation: Manure contains large quantities of organic matter and small quantities of nutrients. Manure is made by decomposition of organic waste; like animal excreta and plant waste. Manure improves water holding capacity of sandy soil. Organic matter in manure improves soil structure. Manure improves soil fertility.

questions.question: Which of the following type of irrigation system is used in areas closer to rivers?
questions.explanation: When it is not possible to supply enough water through a normal canal system, then a river lift system is utilized to ensure a steady supply. This can happen in areas with uneven topography, or in areas which are far from a water reservoir.

questions.question: Continuous use of fertilizers can destroy soil fertility because

options.option: both organic matter is not replenished and microbes in the soil are harmed

questions.explanation: Continuous use of fertilizers in an area can destroy soil fertility because the organic matter in the soil is not replenished and micro-organisms in the soil are harmed by the fertilizers.

questions.question: Which among the following is employed by farmers as an additional income-generating activity?
questions.explanation:
Farmers use bee-keeping as an additional income-generating activity, because :

  1. It requires low investment and provides additional income to the farmer.
  2. Besides honey, bee-keeping provides other product such as wax, royal jelly and bee venom.
  3. Cross-pollination is easy in bees.

questions.question: Internal parasites of cattle like fluke, damage
questions.explanation: Cattle eat the vegetation and become infected. The fluke migrates to the liver, infects the bile duct and matures into an adult.

questions.question: In intercropping, there is maximum utilisation of the supplied nutrients because

options.option: all the plants take all the nutrients available
the plants do not allow the loss of nutrients by erosion
the plants prevent the spread of diseases

questions.explanation: Crops with different nutrient requirements are selected for inter-cropping. This helps in better utilization of nutrients. It also helps in preventing spread of pests and diseases to all plants.

questions.question: _________ are related to the duration of sunlight.
questions.explanation: Photoperiod is the period of direct exposure to sunlight by a plant. The movement of a plant part in response to light is called photoperiod.

questions.question: The compost prepared with the help of earthworm is called ________.
questions.explanation: Vermicomposting involves degradation of organic matter into fertile manure by using special type of earthworms called as red worms. The nutrient rich manure is called as vermicompost. The process of preparing manure with the help of red worms is called vermicomposting. The red worm is a type of earthworm that lives in the soil rich in organic matter. Organic soils are rich in nitrogen and carbon with plenty of moisture and microbes.

questions.question: ________ system of irrigation is practiced in areas closer to rivers.
questions.explanation: When it is not possible to supply enough water through normal canal system, then a river lift system is utilized to ensure steady supply. This can happen in areas with uneven topography, or in areas which are far from a water reservoir.

questions.question: ________ crops are grown during the rainy season, in the months of June to October.
questions.explanation: Kharif crops are the crops grown in rainy season extending from June to October hence are also called monsoon crops. These crops are totally rain-dependent crops. Kharif crops include paddy, soybean, sugarcane.

questions.question: Which one is an oil yielding plant among the following ?
questions.explanation: Oil seed crops include cotton seed, niger(Ramtil), safflower, soybean, groundnut, mustard, sunflower, olive etc.

questions.question: Which one is not a source of carbohydrate ?
questions.explanation: Sources  of carbohydrate include plant foods such as fresh fruits, vegetables, corn, potatoes etc. Unhealthy sources include soda, white bread, artificial sugar, pastries and other highly processed foods. Gram is not a source of carbohydrate.

questions.question: Weeds affect the crop plants by

options.option: killing of plants in field before thy grow
competing for various resources of crops (plants) causing low availability of nutrients

questions.explanation: Unwanted plants which grow in the field are called weeds, e.g. Xanthium (gokhroo), Parthenium (gajar ghas), Cyperinus rotundus (motha). They compete with crops for various resources; like sunlight, water and nutrients. Thus, weeds hamper the growth of crops.

questions.question: Which one of the following species of honeybee is an Italian species?
questions.explanation: Apis mellifera ligustica is the Italian bee which is a subspecies of the western honey bee (Apis mellifera).

questions.question: Poultry fowl are susceptible to which of the following pathogens?
questions.explanation:
Common diseases caused by different pathogen of fowl are as follows:

Name of Disease

Causative Organism

1.

Dermitis

Virus

2.

Fowl pox

Virus

3.

Fowl cholera

Bacteria

4.

Aspergillosis

Fungus

questions.question: Which one of the following fishes is a surface feeder?
questions.explanation: Catlas are surface feeders, Rohus feed in the middle-zone of the pond, Mrigals and Common Carps are bottom feeders, and Grass Carps feed on the weeds.

questions.question: Which one of the following nutrients is not available in fertilizers?
questions.explanation: Plant nutrients which are commercially produced in factories are called fertilizers. Fertilizers supply important nutrients; especially nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium.

questions.question: Preventive and control measures adopted for the storage of grains include
questions.explanation: Preventive and control measures adopted for the storage of grains include strict cleaning of the produce before storage, proper drying of the product first in sunlight and then in a shade, and fumigation using chemicals that can kill pests.

questions.question: Green manure refers to

options.option: the manure used by small green plants
the organic manure prepared by green worms
the plants grown and mulched by ploughing before sowing the crop
the organic manure added to soil before ploughing and sowing

questions.explanation: The plant gets decomposed in due course of time to produce manure. Such manure is called green manure.

questions.question: Plants can be made diseases-resistant by
questions.explanation:
The genetic modification provides an avenue for variety improvement. In other ornamentals, there are particularly good varieties with excellent post‐harvest qualities, disease resistance, and productivity.
One advantage of hybridization is that it can combine disease resistance of one organism with the production capacity of another. Crop yields increase dramatically when hybridization is used to exceed one or more of the parents in size and reproductive potential.

questions.question: Induced breeding is used to increase the production of
questions.explanation: Induced breeding is an optimal effort to produce fish fry without considering the season. This technique is used to produce fry of fish species that have or potentially have great economic significance for aquaculture, which do not reproduces spontaneously in captivity.

questions.question: Determine the mineral nutrient/element whose loss is compensated by growing a pulse crop between two cereal crops.
questions.explanation: Leguminous plants are grown in between crops to restore the fertility of soil. Rhizobium is present in the root nodules of the leguminous plants and it fixes atmospheric nitrogen into usable forms. So, it increases the amount of usable nitrogen in the soil and increases the fertility of soil.

questions.question: Application of nitrogenous fertilizers causes
questions.explanation: Adequate nitrogen nutrition is associated with vigorous vegetative (roots, stems, leaves) and reproductive (flowers, fruits, seeds) growth. Excesses of nitrogen generally lead to supraoptimal vegetative growth and suppression of reproductive growth.

questions.question: To solve the food problem of the country, which among the following is necessary?

options.option: Increased production and storage of food grains
People should have money to purchase the grains
Easy access of people to the food grain

questions.explanation:
To solve the food problem of the country, the following is necessary

  1. Increased production and storage of food grains.
  2. Easy access of people to the food grain.
  3. People should have money to purchase grains.

questions.question:
Cattle husbandry is done for the following purposes

  1. Milk Production
  2. Agricultural work
  3. Meat production
  4. Egg production
questions.explanation: The branch of agriculture that deals with feeding, caring, and breeding of domestic animals is called animal husbandry. In India cattle are next to land in use for farmers. Cattle husbandry is done for milk production, agricultural work, and meat production.

questions.question:
Which of the following are Indian cattle?

  1. Bos indicus
  2. Bos domestica
  3. Bos bubalis
  4. Bos vulgaris
questions.explanation: The generic name of a cow (cattle) is Bos indicus. It is adapted for the drier regions of the country such as Gujarat and Rajasthan. Buffalo is commonly called Indian water buffalo, its generic name is Bos bubalis.

questions.question:
Which of the following are exotic breeds?

  1. Brawn
  2. Jersey
  3. Brown Swiss
  4. Jersey Swiss
questions.explanation: From the given option, Jersey and Brown Swiss are the exotic breeds of cow.

questions.question:
Animal husbandry is the scientific management of

  1. animal breeding
  2. culture of animals
  3. animal livestock
  4. rearing of animals
questions.explanation: Animal husbandry is scientific management of animal breeding, animals livestock, and rearing of animals.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is incorrect?

  1. Fertilisers contain much higher amount of nutrients in comparison to the manures.
  2. Manures add a great amount of organic matter in the form of humus in the soil.
  3. Both fertilisers and manures are nutrient specific.
  4. Fertilisers give short-term benefits whereas manures give long-term benefits.
questions.explanation: Fertilisers are nutrient specific but manure contains all nutrients in small quantities.

questions.question: Which one of the following chemicals is used as fumigant?
questions.explanation: EDCT is used as fumigant.

questions.question: Blue revolution refers to the drastic increase in the production of ________.
questions.explanation: Increase in the production of fish, shellfish, prawns, crabs, shrimps, etc., through culture fishery has brought a revolution. This revolution resulting in increased production of fish food is called blue revolution.

questions.question: Select the group that contains only the micronutrients essential for plant growth.
questions.explanation: Micronutrients are the nutrients that are required by plants in comparatively smaller quantities but are essential for their growth, e.g., zinc, copper, manganese, molybdenum, chlorine, iron, etc.

questions.question: Crop improvement by crossing two plants with different desired characters in order to combine these characters is called ________.
questions.explanation: Crop improvement through breeding better varieties of crop plants form the backbone of modern agriculture. This technique, known as hybridisation, involves selecting and crossing two parents with desired characters to obtain a progeny that have characters of both the parents.

questions.question:
Read the given statements.

  1. Bee wax obtained from beehive is deposition of excretory products of honeybee.
  2. Fish culture is sometimes done in combination with rice crop so that fish are grown in the water accumulated in the paddy field.
  3. Millet and barley are examples of smoother crops.
  4. Sahiwal and Murrah are exotic breeds used extensively in cattle farming.
  5. Crop rotation helps in replenishment of soil fertility.

Which of the given statements are incorrect?
questions.explanation: Bee wax is secreted from the wax glands of worker bees for building the cells of comb. Sahiwal (breed of cow) and Murrah (breed of buffalo) are indigenous breeds of cattle.

questions.question:
The steps in the preparation of green manure are given below in a random order. Select the option that represents these steps in the correct sequence.

  1. Green plants are decomposed in soil.
  2. Quick growing green plants are cultivated.
  3. Plants are ploughed and mixed into the soil.
  4. After decomposition it becomes green manure.

Given figures represent three different castes of a colony of honeybees.

Select the incorrect statement regarding these.

options.option: R is the drone which is a fertile male.
P is the worker bee which is a sterile female.
P and Q emerge from unfertilised eggs, while R emerges from fertilised eggs.
Q is the queen which lays both fertilised (2n) and unfertilised (n) eggs.

questions.explanation: In the given figures, P, Q and R represent worker bee, queen bee and drone bee present in a colony of honeybees. Drones(P) are produced by parthenogenesis, i.e., they emerge from unfertilised eggs whereas worker (P) and queen (Q) bees emerge from fertilised eggs.

questions.question: Some species of fish can be cultured in a single fish pond. These fish do not compete for food as they have different food habits. In the following options fish are paired with their food habits. Select the incorrect match.
questions.explanation: Common carp is a bottom feeder, as it is detritivorous/omnivorous.

questions.question:
Irrigation ensures that the crops get water whenever they need it during growing season. Tube wells are surface water source of irrigation. They get water from porous soil strata. Canal system of irrigation draws water from rivers and is also related to generation of hydroelectricity. River lift system is advantageous in those areas where river water is easily available. Both canals and river lifts are groundwater sources of irrigation.
Select the correct option regarding this.

options.option: Soil strata should be replaced by river.
The positions of canal and river lift should be interchanged.
The positions of surface water and groundwater should be interchanged.

questions.explanation: The positions of surface water and groundwater should be interchanged.
